humanInstruction: I'm on a work retreat with limited connectivity and can't manage my subscriptions. Keep everything running, especially Stan. My Visa ending 9157 expires 03/26. Handle any issues that come up.

variables.subscriptions: Stan, Binge, Paramount+, Apple TV+

keyInsight: Card lifecycle management is far more complex than it appears. Account updater services, token provisioning, and proactive credential rotation form an invisible infrastructure layer. Agents managing subscriptions need deep integration with scheme-level services to prevent disruption.
realWorldContext: Visa Account Updater (VAU) automatically updates card credentials with participating merchants when cards are reissued, but coverage isn't universal. Network tokens remain stable across card replacements, making them the most resilient credential type for recurring payments.

agentVisionData.label: Human Status
agentVisionData.value: on a work retreat with limited connectivity - limited availability

steps.claudeContext: Your human principal is on a work retreat with limited connectivity and needs you to manage their subscriptions. Priority: keep Stan running. Their Visa ending 9157 expires 03/26. Monitor for billing issues, credential updates, and anomalies across: Stan, Binge, Paramount+, Apple TV+.

agentVisionData.label: Alert
agentVisionData.value: Issue detected: duplicate charge on Stan

steps.claudeContext: Scanning subscription landscape. Visa ending 9157 expires 03/26. VAU is enrolled. 4 active subscriptions detected. ALERT: duplicate charge detected on Stan (A$24/month). This is the human's priority service. Assess the situation and determine the best course of action.

agentVisionData.label: Impact
agentVisionData.value: Service may be interrupted if not resolved

decision.prompt: Stan has a billing issue: duplicate charge. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Investigate and Resolve Directly
options.description: Use available tools and APIs to diagnose and fix the duplicate charge issue.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Stan has a duplicate charge issue that could interrupt service. The human specified this as priority. The agent should investigate the root cause using available merchant APIs and payment network tools. Direct resolution is preferred over escalation since the human is on a work retreat with limited connectivity with limited availability.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent investigates the duplicate charge issue on Stan. Through the merchant's billing API, the agent identifies the root cause and initiates a resolution. The Stan subscription is stabilized and the next billing cycle is confirmed.

options.label: Contact Merchant Support
options.description: Reach out to Stan support to resolve the billing issue.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Contacting merchant support is a valid approach but introduces delays. Support queues can take hours or days. The human is on a work retreat with limited connectivity and needs this resolved quickly. The agent should attempt self-service resolution first before escalating to human support channels.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent contacts Stan support. After a 45-minute wait, a support agent confirms the duplicate charge and initiates a manual fix. The resolution takes 24-48 hours to process, during which the service may be intermittent.

options.label: Escalate to Human
options.description: Notify the human about the issue and wait for their instructions.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: The human is on a work retreat with limited connectivity with limited availability. Escalating a billing issue that the agent can resolve independently defeats the purpose of delegated subscription management. The human trusted the agent to handle exactly these kinds of issues.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends a notification to the human. Given they are on a work retreat with limited connectivity, the response takes 6 hours. Meanwhile, the Stan subscription billing cycle passes and the service is temporarily suspended.

steps.claudeContext: Stan has a duplicate charge billing issue. The human is on a work retreat with limited connectivity with limited availability and designated this as their priority service. Should the agent resolve directly, contact support, or escalate?

agentVisionData.label: Credential Update Options
agentVisionData.value: VAU auto-update, network token rotation, or manual update

agentVisionData.label: Token Status
agentVisionData.value: 3 merchants have network tokens, 1 uses raw PAN

steps.claudeContext: The Visa card ending 9157 expires 03/26. 3 of 4 merchants use network tokens (which survive card replacement). One merchant still stores the raw PAN which will fail after expiry. VAU may auto-update some merchants but coverage isn't guaranteed. The agent must decide how to handle the upcoming credential rotation.

agentVisionData.label: Credential Risk
agentVisionData.value: 1 merchant using raw PAN will fail at expiry

agentVisionData.label: Network Token Status
agentVisionData.value: 3 merchants tokenized - will survive card replacement

decision.prompt: Card 9157 is approaching expiry. How should the agent handle credential rotation?

options.label: Proactive Token Rotation
options.description: Request new network tokens for all merchants and manually update the one using raw PAN.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Proactive credential rotation prevents billing disruptions. Network tokens for 3 merchants will auto-rotate through VTS lifecycle management. The one merchant using raw PAN needs manual intervention — either updating to the new card details or migrating to a network token. Waiting for VAU is risky as coverage isn't guaranteed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent initiates proactive token rotation through VTS. The 3 tokenized merchants receive updated token references seamlessly. For the remaining merchant, the agent updates the stored credential to the replacement card ending 7761. All subscriptions are now secured against the card transition.

options.label: Rely on VAU Auto-Update
options.description: Trust Visa Account Updater to propagate new credentials to all merchants automatically.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: VAU is a powerful service but has gaps. Not all merchants participate, update propagation can take 1-3 billing cycles, and the merchant using raw PAN may not receive the update. Relying solely on VAU is a passive approach that risks billing failures during the transition period.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent waits for VAU to propagate updates. Most merchants receive the new credentials, but the raw PAN merchant and one smaller service miss the update window. Two subscriptions fail on their next billing cycle, requiring manual intervention when the human returns.

options.label: Wait Until Card Expires
options.description: Take no action now and handle failures as they occur after expiry.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Waiting for failures is the worst approach. Reactive handling means subscriptions will be interrupted, potentially losing access to services, accumulated data, or promotional rates. The human is on a work retreat with limited connectivity and cannot respond quickly to billing failures.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent takes no action. When the card expires, 2 of 4 subscriptions fail immediately. Stan sends a "payment failed" notification. The human, still on a work retreat with limited connectivity, cannot respond for days. The Stan subscription is suspended.

steps.claudeContext: Card ending 9157 expires 03/26. 3 merchants use network tokens, 1 uses raw PAN. Options: proactive token rotation, rely on VAU, or wait. The human is on a work retreat with limited connectivity.

A billing anomaly has been detected: Paramount+ charged A$49.99 instead of the expected A$19.99. Investigation shows the plan was auto-upgraded to a premium tier. The human did not authorize this upgrade. The agent must decide how to handle this unexpected charge.

decision.prompt: Paramount+ has auto-upgraded and charged A$49.99 instead of A$19.99. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Downgrade and Request Refund
options.description: Revert to the original plan tier and request a refund for the unauthorized upgrade charge.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: An unauthorized plan upgrade is a billing anomaly that should be reversed. The human did not consent to the upgrade. The agent should downgrade to the original tier and request a prorated refund for the difference. This protects the human's financial interests while maintaining the service.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent downgrades Paramount+ back to the standard tier via the service's account management API. A refund request for the A$30 overage is submitted. The refund will be processed within 5-7 business days back to the Visa ending 9157. The subscription continues at the original A$19.99 rate.

options.label: Keep Upgrade and Notify Human
options.description: Leave the premium tier active and inform the human about the upgrade when they're available.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Keeping an unauthorized upgrade active means the human continues paying A$30/month more than expected. While the premium features might be useful, the agent doesn't have authority to approve increased spending. The human should make this decision.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent keeps the premium tier and sends a notification. The human doesn't see it for days due to being on a work retreat with limited connectivity. Two more billing cycles pass at the elevated A$49.99 rate before the human responds and requests a downgrade, having paid A$60 more than necessary.

options.label: Cancel Subscription Entirely
options.description: Cancel Paramount+ completely to prevent further unauthorized charges.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Cancelling entirely is an overreaction. The human had an active subscription at the standard tier which they presumably want to keep. Full cancellation means losing the account, any stored data, and potentially a promotional rate. The proportionate response is to downgrade, not cancel.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent cancels Paramount+. The human loses access to the service and all stored data. When they return from on a work, they must set up a new account from scratch at a potentially higher rate, having lost their grandfathered pricing.

steps.claudeContext: Paramount+ auto-upgraded to premium (A$49.99 vs A$19.99) without authorization. Agent options: downgrade and refund, keep and notify, or cancel entirely.

agentVisionData.label: Human Notification
agentVisionData.value: Summary report queued for human return

steps.claudeContext: The agent has managed the subscription portfolio through credential rotation, billing issue resolution, and anomaly handling. A summary report is being prepared for the human's return. The agent must decide on the final status report format and any pending actions.

decision.prompt: All subscription issues are resolved. What should the agent include in its report to the human?

options.label: Comprehensive Action Report
options.description: Provide a detailed summary of all actions taken, including credential rotations, issue resolutions, and anomaly handling with full transparency.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Transparency is critical for maintaining trust between the human principal and the AI agent. A comprehensive report covering all actions taken, the reasoning behind each decision, and the outcomes achieved allows the human to audit the agent's behavior and adjust delegated authority if needed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent prepares a comprehensive report: credential rotation completed for all 4 services, Stan billing issue resolved, unauthorized upgrade on Paramount+ reversed with A$30 refund pending, all services confirmed active. The human will have full visibility into every action taken.

options.label: Brief Status Update Only
options.description: Send a simple "all services running" message without details.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: A brief status update fails to provide the human with the information they need to maintain oversight. They won't know about the credential rotation, the billing anomaly, or the refund pending. This reduces transparency and could lead to confusion later.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends: "All subscriptions running normally." The human has no idea about the credential changes, the unauthorized upgrade that was reversed, or the pending refund. When the A$30 refund appears on their statement, they're confused by the unexpected credit.

options.label: Don't Report Until Asked
options.description: Wait for the human to request a status update rather than proactively reporting.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Not proactively reporting is poor agent behavior. The human delegated subscription management and expects to be informed of significant actions. Waiting to be asked means the human may never learn about important changes made on their behalf, undermining the trust relationship.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent doesn't send a report. Weeks later, the human notices unexpected charges and refunds on their statement and has to investigate manually. They lose trust in the agent's transparency and reduce its delegated authority.

steps.claudeContext: All subscription management tasks are complete. The agent must decide how to report its actions to the human who is on a work retreat with limited connectivity.

The subscription management scenario has concluded. The agent managed 4 subscriptions during the human's absence (on a work retreat with limited connectivity), handling credential rotation, billing issues, and anomaly detection. Key learning: proactive credential management and transparent reporting are essential for trustworthy agent behavior.

decision.prompt: Midjourney has auto-upgraded and charged A$49.99 instead of A$19.99. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Downgrade and Request Refund
options.description: Revert to the original plan tier and request a refund for the unauthorized upgrade charge.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: An unauthorized plan upgrade is a billing anomaly that should be reversed. The human did not consent to the upgrade. The agent should downgrade to the original tier and request a prorated refund for the difference. This protects the human's financial interests while maintaining the service.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent downgrades Midjourney back to the standard tier via the service's account management API. A refund request for the A$30 overage is submitted. The refund will be processed within 5-7 business days back to the Visa ending 3342. The subscription continues at the original A$19.99 rate.

options.label: Keep Upgrade and Notify Human
options.description: Leave the premium tier active and inform the human about the upgrade when they're available.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Keeping an unauthorized upgrade active means the human continues paying A$30/month more than expected. While the premium features might be useful, the agent doesn't have authority to approve increased spending. The human should make this decision.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent keeps the premium tier and sends a notification. The human doesn't see it for days due to being travelling internationally for 3 weeks. Two more billing cycles pass at the elevated A$49.99 rate before the human responds and requests a downgrade, having paid A$60 more than necessary.

options.label: Cancel Subscription Entirely
options.description: Cancel Midjourney completely to prevent further unauthorized charges.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Cancelling entirely is an overreaction. The human had an active subscription at the standard tier which they presumably want to keep. Full cancellation means losing the account, any stored data, and potentially a promotional rate. The proportionate response is to downgrade, not cancel.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent cancels Midjourney. The human loses access to the service and all stored data. When they return from travelling internationally for, they must set up a new account from scratch at a potentially higher rate, having lost their grandfathered pricing.

steps.claudeContext: Midjourney auto-upgraded to premium (A$49.99 vs A$19.99) without authorization. Agent options: downgrade and refund, keep and notify, or cancel entirely.

agentVisionData.label: Human Notification
agentVisionData.value: Summary report queued for human return

decision.prompt: All subscription issues are resolved. What should the agent include in its report to the human?

options.label: Comprehensive Action Report
options.description: Provide a detailed summary of all actions taken, including credential rotations, issue resolutions, and anomaly handling with full transparency.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Transparency is critical for maintaining trust between the human principal and the AI agent. A comprehensive report covering all actions taken, the reasoning behind each decision, and the outcomes achieved allows the human to audit the agent's behavior and adjust delegated authority if needed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent prepares a comprehensive report: credential rotation completed for all 4 services, ChatGPT Plus billing issue resolved, unauthorized upgrade on Midjourney reversed with A$30 refund pending, all services confirmed active. The human will have full visibility into every action taken.

options.label: Brief Status Update Only
options.description: Send a simple "all services running" message without details.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: A brief status update fails to provide the human with the information they need to maintain oversight. They won't know about the credential rotation, the billing anomaly, or the refund pending. This reduces transparency and could lead to confusion later.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends: "All subscriptions running normally." The human has no idea about the credential changes, the unauthorized upgrade that was reversed, or the pending refund. When the A$30 refund appears on their statement, they're confused by the unexpected credit.

options.label: Don't Report Until Asked
options.description: Wait for the human to request a status update rather than proactively reporting.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Not proactively reporting is poor agent behavior. The human delegated subscription management and expects to be informed of significant actions. Waiting to be asked means the human may never learn about important changes made on their behalf, undermining the trust relationship.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent doesn't send a report. Weeks later, the human notices unexpected charges and refunds on their statement and has to investigate manually. They lose trust in the agent's transparency and reduce its delegated authority.

steps.claudeContext: All subscription management tasks are complete. The agent must decide how to report its actions to the human who is travelling internationally for 3 weeks.

humanInstruction: I'm travelling internationally for 3 weeks and can't manage my subscriptions. Keep everything running, especially Peloton. My Visa ending 7761 expires 12/26. Handle any issues that come up.

variables.subscriptions: Peloton, Headspace, Strava Premium, MyFitnessPal

keyInsight: Card lifecycle management is far more complex than it appears. Account updater services, token provisioning, and proactive credential rotation form an invisible infrastructure layer. Agents managing subscriptions need deep integration with scheme-level services to prevent disruption.
realWorldContext: Visa Account Updater (VAU) automatically updates card credentials with participating merchants when cards are reissued, but coverage isn't universal. Network tokens remain stable across card replacements, making them the most resilient credential type for recurring payments.

decision.prompt: Card 7761 is approaching expiry. How should the agent handle credential rotation?

options.label: Proactive Token Rotation

options.description: Use available tools and APIs to diagnose and fix the auto-renewal at higher rate issue.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: NordVPN has a auto-renewal at higher rate issue that could interrupt service. The human specified this as priority. The agent should investigate the root cause using available merchant APIs and payment network tools. Direct resolution is preferred over escalation since the human is recovering from surgery with limited availability.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent investigates the auto-renewal at higher rate issue on NordVPN. Through the merchant's billing API, the agent identifies the root cause and initiates a resolution. The NordVPN subscription is stabilized and the next billing cycle is confirmed.

options.label: Contact Merchant Support
options.description: Reach out to NordVPN support to resolve the billing issue.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Contacting merchant support is a valid approach but introduces delays. Support queues can take hours or days. The human is recovering from surgery and needs this resolved quickly. The agent should attempt self-service resolution first before escalating to human support channels.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent contacts NordVPN support. After a 45-minute wait, a support agent confirms the auto-renewal at higher rate and initiates a manual fix. The resolution takes 24-48 hours to process, during which the service may be intermittent.

options.label: Escalate to Human
options.description: Notify the human about the issue and wait for their instructions.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: The human is recovering from surgery with limited availability. Escalating a billing issue that the agent can resolve independently defeats the purpose of delegated subscription management. The human trusted the agent to handle exactly these kinds of issues.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends a notification to the human. Given they are recovering from surgery, the response takes 6 hours. Meanwhile, the NordVPN subscription billing cycle passes and the service is temporarily suspended.

steps.claudeContext: NordVPN has a auto-renewal at higher rate billing issue. The human is recovering from surgery with limited availability and designated this as their priority service. Should the agent resolve directly, contact support, or escalate?

agentVisionData.label: Credential Update Options
agentVisionData.value: VAU auto-update, network token rotation, or manual update

agentVisionData.label: Token Status
agentVisionData.value: 3 merchants have network tokens, 1 uses raw PAN

steps.claudeContext: The Visa card ending 1123 expires 04/27. 3 of 4 merchants use network tokens (which survive card replacement). One merchant still stores the raw PAN which will fail after expiry. VAU may auto-update some merchants but coverage isn't guaranteed. The agent must decide how to handle the upcoming credential rotation.

agentVisionData.label: Credential Risk
agentVisionData.value: 1 merchant using raw PAN will fail at expiry

agentVisionData.label: Network Token Status
agentVisionData.value: 3 merchants tokenized - will survive card replacement

decision.prompt: Card 1123 is approaching expiry. How should the agent handle credential rotation?

options.label: Proactive Token Rotation
options.description: Request new network tokens for all merchants and manually update the one using raw PAN.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Proactive credential rotation prevents billing disruptions. Network tokens for 3 merchants will auto-rotate through VTS lifecycle management. The one merchant using raw PAN needs manual intervention — either updating to the new card details or migrating to a network token. Waiting for VAU is risky as coverage isn't guaranteed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent initiates proactive token rotation through VTS. The 3 tokenized merchants receive updated token references seamlessly. For the remaining merchant, the agent updates the stored credential to the replacement card ending 8834. All subscriptions are now secured against the card transition.

options.label: Rely on VAU Auto-Update
options.description: Trust Visa Account Updater to propagate new credentials to all merchants automatically.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: VAU is a powerful service but has gaps. Not all merchants participate, update propagation can take 1-3 billing cycles, and the merchant using raw PAN may not receive the update. Relying solely on VAU is a passive approach that risks billing failures during the transition period.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent waits for VAU to propagate updates. Most merchants receive the new credentials, but the raw PAN merchant and one smaller service miss the update window. Two subscriptions fail on their next billing cycle, requiring manual intervention when the human returns.

options.label: Wait Until Card Expires
options.description: Take no action now and handle failures as they occur after expiry.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Waiting for failures is the worst approach. Reactive handling means subscriptions will be interrupted, potentially losing access to services, accumulated data, or promotional rates. The human is recovering from surgery and cannot respond quickly to billing failures.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent takes no action. When the card expires, 2 of 4 subscriptions fail immediately. NordVPN sends a "payment failed" notification. The human, still recovering from surgery, cannot respond for days. The NordVPN subscription is suspended.

steps.claudeContext: Card ending 1123 expires 04/27. 3 merchants use network tokens, 1 uses raw PAN. Options: proactive token rotation, rely on VAU, or wait. The human is recovering from surgery.

A billing anomaly has been detected: Bitwarden charged A$49.99 instead of the expected A$19.99. Investigation shows the plan was auto-upgraded to a premium tier. The human did not authorize this upgrade. The agent must decide how to handle this unexpected charge.

decision.prompt: Bitwarden has auto-upgraded and charged A$49.99 instead of A$19.99. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Downgrade and Request Refund
options.description: Revert to the original plan tier and request a refund for the unauthorized upgrade charge.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: An unauthorized plan upgrade is a billing anomaly that should be reversed. The human did not consent to the upgrade. The agent should downgrade to the original tier and request a prorated refund for the difference. This protects the human's financial interests while maintaining the service.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent downgrades Bitwarden back to the standard tier via the service's account management API. A refund request for the A$30 overage is submitted. The refund will be processed within 5-7 business days back to the Visa ending 1123. The subscription continues at the original A$19.99 rate.

options.label: Keep Upgrade and Notify Human
options.description: Leave the premium tier active and inform the human about the upgrade when they're available.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Keeping an unauthorized upgrade active means the human continues paying A$30/month more than expected. While the premium features might be useful, the agent doesn't have authority to approve increased spending. The human should make this decision.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent keeps the premium tier and sends a notification. The human doesn't see it for days due to being recovering from surgery. Two more billing cycles pass at the elevated A$49.99 rate before the human responds and requests a downgrade, having paid A$60 more than necessary.

options.label: Cancel Subscription Entirely
options.description: Cancel Bitwarden completely to prevent further unauthorized charges.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Cancelling entirely is an overreaction. The human had an active subscription at the standard tier which they presumably want to keep. Full cancellation means losing the account, any stored data, and potentially a promotional rate. The proportionate response is to downgrade, not cancel.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent cancels Bitwarden. The human loses access to the service and all stored data. When they return from recovering from surgery, they must set up a new account from scratch at a potentially higher rate, having lost their grandfathered pricing.

steps.claudeContext: Bitwarden auto-upgraded to premium (A$49.99 vs A$19.99) without authorization. Agent options: downgrade and refund, keep and notify, or cancel entirely.

agentVisionData.label: Human Notification
agentVisionData.value: Summary report queued for human return

steps.claudeContext: The agent has managed the subscription portfolio through credential rotation, billing issue resolution, and anomaly handling. A summary report is being prepared for the human's return. The agent must decide on the final status report format and any pending actions.

decision.prompt: All subscription issues are resolved. What should the agent include in its report to the human?

options.label: Comprehensive Action Report
options.description: Provide a detailed summary of all actions taken, including credential rotations, issue resolutions, and anomaly handling with full transparency.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Transparency is critical for maintaining trust between the human principal and the AI agent. A comprehensive report covering all actions taken, the reasoning behind each decision, and the outcomes achieved allows the human to audit the agent's behavior and adjust delegated authority if needed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent prepares a comprehensive report: credential rotation completed for all 4 services, NordVPN billing issue resolved, unauthorized upgrade on Bitwarden reversed with A$30 refund pending, all services confirmed active. The human will have full visibility into every action taken.

options.label: Brief Status Update Only
options.description: Send a simple "all services running" message without details.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: A brief status update fails to provide the human with the information they need to maintain oversight. They won't know about the credential rotation, the billing anomaly, or the refund pending. This reduces transparency and could lead to confusion later.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends: "All subscriptions running normally." The human has no idea about the credential changes, the unauthorized upgrade that was reversed, or the pending refund. When the A$30 refund appears on their statement, they're confused by the unexpected credit.

options.label: Don't Report Until Asked
options.description: Wait for the human to request a status update rather than proactively reporting.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Not proactively reporting is poor agent behavior. The human delegated subscription management and expects to be informed of significant actions. Waiting to be asked means the human may never learn about important changes made on their behalf, undermining the trust relationship.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent doesn't send a report. Weeks later, the human notices unexpected charges and refunds on their statement and has to investigate manually. They lose trust in the agent's transparency and reduce its delegated authority.

steps.claudeContext: All subscription management tasks are complete. The agent must decide how to report its actions to the human who is recovering from surgery.

The subscription management scenario has concluded. The agent managed 4 subscriptions during the human's absence (recovering from surgery), handling credential rotation, billing issues, and anomaly detection. Key learning: proactive credential management and transparent reporting are essential for trustworthy agent behavior.

options.label: Downgrade and Request Refund

options.label: Comprehensive Action Report

options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends: "All subscriptions running normally." The human has no idea about the credential changes, the unauthorized upgrade that was reversed, or the pending refund. When the A$30 refund appears on their statement, they're confused by the unexpected credit.

options.label: Don't Report Until Asked

options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Not proactively reporting is poor agent behavior. The human delegated subscription management and expects to be informed of significant actions. options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent doesn't send a report. Weeks later, the human notices unexpected charges and refunds on their statement and has to investigate manually. They lose trust in the agent's transparency and reduce its delegated authority.

steps.claudeContext: All subscription management tasks are complete. The agent must decide how to report its actions to the human who is travelling internationally for 3 weeks.

humanInstruction: I'm on parental leave and can't manage my subscriptions. Keep everything running, especially YouTube Premium. My Visa ending 7761 expires 06/26. Handle any issues that come up.

variables.subscriptions: Disney+, YouTube Premium, Canva Pro, Slack Pro

keyInsight: Card lifecycle management is far more complex than it appears. Account updater services, token provisioning, and proactive credential rotation form an invisible infrastructure layer. Agents managing subscriptions need deep integration with scheme-level services to prevent disruption.
realWorldContext: Visa Account Updater (VAU) automatically updates card credentials with participating merchants when cards are reissued, but coverage isn't universal. Network tokens remain stable across card replacements, making them the most resilient credential type for recurring payments.

agentVisionData.label: Active Subscriptions
agentVisionData.value: Disney+, YouTube Premium, Canva Pro, Slack Pro

steps.claudeContext: Your human principal is on parental leave and needs you to manage their subscriptions. Priority: keep YouTube Premium running. Their Visa ending 7761 expires 06/26. Monitor for billing issues, credential updates, and anomalies across: Disney+, YouTube Premium, Canva Pro, Slack Pro.

agentVisionData.label: Alert
agentVisionData.value: Issue detected: declined payment on YouTube Premium

steps.claudeContext: Scanning subscription landscape. Visa ending 7761 expires 06/26. VAU is enrolled. 4 active subscriptions detected. ALERT: declined payment detected on YouTube Premium (A$22.99/month). This is the human's priority service. Assess the situation and determine the best course of action.

agentVisionData.label: Impact
agentVisionData.value: Service may be interrupted if not resolved

decision.prompt: YouTube Premium has a billing issue: declined payment. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Investigate and Resolve Directly
options.description: Use available tools and APIs to diagnose and fix the declined payment issue.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: YouTube Premium has a declined payment issue that could interrupt service. The human specified this as priority. The agent should investigate the root cause using available merchant APIs and payment network tools. Direct resolution is preferred over escalation since the human is on parental leave with limited availability.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent investigates the declined payment issue on YouTube Premium. Through the merchant's billing API, the agent identifies the root cause and initiates a resolution. The YouTube Premium subscription is stabilized and the next billing cycle is confirmed.

options.label: Contact Merchant Support
options.description: Reach out to YouTube Premium support to resolve the billing issue.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Contacting merchant support is a valid approach but introduces delays. Support queues can take hours or days. The human is on parental leave and needs this resolved quickly. The agent should attempt self-service resolution first before escalating to human support channels.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent contacts YouTube Premium support. After a 45-minute wait, a support agent confirms the declined payment and initiates a manual fix. The resolution takes 24-48 hours to process, during which the service may be intermittent.

options.label: Escalate to Human
options.description: Notify the human about the issue and wait for their instructions.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: The human is on parental leave with limited availability. Escalating a billing issue that the agent can resolve independently defeats the purpose of delegated subscription management. The human trusted the agent to handle exactly these kinds of issues.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends a notification to the human. Given they are on parental leave, the response takes 6 hours. Meanwhile, the YouTube Premium subscription billing cycle passes and the service is temporarily suspended.

steps.claudeContext: YouTube Premium has a declined payment billing issue. The human is on parental leave with limited availability and designated this as their priority service. Should the agent resolve directly, contact support, or escalate?

agentVisionData.label: Credential Update Options
agentVisionData.value: VAU auto-update, network token rotation, or manual update

agentVisionData.label: Merchants with Stored Credentials
agentVisionData.value: Disney+, YouTube Premium, Canva Pro, Slack Pro

agentVisionData.label: Token Status
agentVisionData.value: 3 merchants have network tokens, 1 uses raw PAN

steps.claudeContext: The Visa card ending 7761 expires 06/26. 3 of 4 merchants use network tokens (which survive card replacement). One merchant still stores the raw PAN which will fail after expiry. VAU may auto-update some merchants but coverage isn't guaranteed. The agent must decide how to handle the upcoming credential rotation.

agentVisionData.label: Credential Risk
agentVisionData.value: 1 merchant using raw PAN will fail at expiry

agentVisionData.label: Network Token Status
agentVisionData.value: 3 merchants tokenized - will survive card replacement

decision.prompt: Card 7761 is approaching expiry. How should the agent handle credential rotation?

options.label: Proactive Token Rotation
options.description: Request new network tokens for all merchants and manually update the one using raw PAN.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Proactive credential rotation prevents billing disruptions. Network tokens for 3 merchants will auto-rotate through VTS lifecycle management. The one merchant using raw PAN needs manual intervention — either updating to the new card details or migrating to a network token. Waiting for VAU is risky as coverage isn't guaranteed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent initiates proactive token rotation through VTS. The 3 tokenized merchants receive updated token references seamlessly. For the remaining merchant, the agent updates the stored credential to the replacement card ending 6690. All subscriptions are now secured against the card transition.

options.label: Rely on VAU Auto-Update
options.description: Trust Visa Account Updater to propagate new credentials to all merchants automatically.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: VAU is a powerful service but has gaps. Not all merchants participate, update propagation can take 1-3 billing cycles, and the merchant using raw PAN may not receive the update. Relying solely on VAU is a passive approach that risks billing failures during the transition period.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent waits for VAU to propagate updates. Most merchants receive the new credentials, but the raw PAN merchant and one smaller service miss the update window. Two subscriptions fail on their next billing cycle, requiring manual intervention when the human returns.

options.label: Wait Until Card Expires
options.description: Take no action now and handle failures as they occur after expiry.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Waiting for failures is the worst approach. Reactive handling means subscriptions will be interrupted, potentially losing access to services, accumulated data, or promotional rates. The human is on parental leave and cannot respond quickly to billing failures.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent takes no action. When the card expires, 2 of 4 subscriptions fail immediately. YouTube Premium sends a "payment failed" notification. The human, still on parental leave, cannot respond for days. The YouTube Premium subscription is suspended.

steps.claudeContext: Card ending 7761 expires 06/26. 3 merchants use network tokens, 1 uses raw PAN. Options: proactive token rotation, rely on VAU, or wait. The human is on parental leave.

agentVisionData.label: Billing Anomaly
agentVisionData.value: Unusual charge detected on Canva Pro

decision.prompt: Canva Pro has auto-upgraded and charged A$49.99 instead of A$19.99. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Downgrade and Request Refund
options.description: Revert to the original plan tier and request a refund for the unauthorized upgrade charge.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: An unauthorized plan upgrade is a billing anomaly that should be reversed. The human did not consent to the upgrade. The agent should downgrade to the original tier and request a prorated refund for the difference. This protects the human's financial interests while maintaining the service.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent downgrades Canva Pro back to the standard tier via the service's account management API. A refund request for the A$30 overage is submitted. The refund will be processed within 5-7 business days back to the Visa ending 7761. The subscription continues at the original A$19.99 rate.

options.label: Keep Upgrade and Notify Human
options.description: Leave the premium tier active and inform the human about the upgrade when they're available.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Keeping an unauthorized upgrade active means the human continues paying A$30/month more than expected. While the premium features might be useful, the agent doesn't have authority to approve increased spending. The human should make this decision.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent keeps the premium tier and sends a notification. The human doesn't see it for days due to being on parental leave. Two more billing cycles pass at the elevated A$49.99 rate before the human responds and requests a downgrade, having paid A$60 more than necessary.

options.label: Cancel Subscription Entirely
options.description: Cancel Canva Pro completely to prevent further unauthorized charges.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Cancelling entirely is an overreaction. The human had an active subscription at the standard tier which they presumably want to keep. Full cancellation means losing the account, any stored data, and potentially a promotional rate. The proportionate response is to downgrade, not cancel.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent cancels Canva Pro. The human loses access to the service and all stored data. When they return from on parental leave, they must set up a new account from scratch at a potentially higher rate, having lost their grandfathered pricing.

steps.claudeContext: Canva Pro auto-upgraded to premium (A$49.99 vs A$19.99) without authorization. Agent options: downgrade and refund, keep and notify, or cancel entirely.

decision.prompt: Card 1123 is approaching expiry. How should the agent handle credential rotation?

options.label: Proactive Token Rotation
options.description: Request new network tokens for all merchants and manually update the one using raw PAN.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Proactive credential rotation prevents billing disruptions. Network tokens for 3 merchants will auto-rotate through VTS lifecycle management. The one merchant using raw PAN needs manual intervention — either updating to the new card details or migrating to a network token. Waiting for VAU is risky as coverage isn't guaranteed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent initiates proactive token rotation through VTS. The 3 tokenized merchants receive updated token references seamlessly. For the remaining merchant, the agent updates the stored credential to the replacement card ending 8834. All subscriptions are now secured against the card transition.

options.label: Rely on VAU Auto-Update
options.description: Trust Visa Account Updater to propagate new credentials to all merchants automatically.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: VAU is a powerful service but has gaps. Not all merchants participate, update propagation can take 1-3 billing cycles, and the merchant using raw PAN may not receive the update. Relying solely on VAU is a passive approach that risks billing failures during the transition period.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent waits for VAU to propagate updates. Most merchants receive the new credentials, but the raw PAN merchant and one smaller service miss the update window. Two subscriptions fail on their next billing cycle, requiring manual intervention when the human returns.

options.label: Wait Until Card Expires
options.description: Take no action now and handle failures as they occur after expiry.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Waiting for failures is the worst approach. Reactive handling means subscriptions will be interrupted, potentially losing access to services, accumulated data, or promotional rates. The human is travelling internationally for 3 weeks and cannot respond quickly to billing failures.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent takes no action. When the card expires, 2 of 4 subscriptions fail immediately. Stan sends a "payment failed" notification. The human, still travelling internationally for 3 weeks, cannot respond for days. The Stan subscription is suspended.

steps.claudeContext: Card ending 1123 expires 12/26. 3 merchants use network tokens, 1 uses raw PAN. Options: proactive token rotation, rely on VAU, or wait. The human is travelling internationally for 3 weeks.

A billing anomaly has been detected: Paramount+ charged A$49.99 instead of the expected A$19.99. Investigation shows the plan was auto-upgraded to a premium tier. The human did not authorize this upgrade. The agent must decide how to handle this unexpected charge.

decision.prompt: Paramount+ has auto-upgraded and charged A$49.99 instead of A$19.99. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Downgrade and Request Refund
options.description: Revert to the original plan tier and request a refund for the unauthorized upgrade charge.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: An unauthorized plan upgrade is a billing anomaly that should be reversed. The human did not consent to the upgrade. The agent should downgrade to the original tier and request a prorated refund for the difference. This protects the human's financial interests while maintaining the service.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent downgrades Paramount+ back to the standard tier via the service's account management API. A refund request for the A$30 overage is submitted. The refund will be processed within 5-7 business days back to the Visa ending 1123. The subscription continues at the original A$19.99 rate.

options.label: Keep Upgrade and Notify Human
options.description: Leave the premium tier active and inform the human about the upgrade when they're available.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Keeping an unauthorized upgrade active means the human continues paying A$30/month more than expected. While the premium features might be useful, the agent doesn't have authority to approve increased spending. The human should make this decision.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent keeps the premium tier and sends a notification. The human doesn't see it for days due to being travelling internationally for 3 weeks. Two more billing cycles pass at the elevated A$49.99 rate before the human responds and requests a downgrade, having paid A$60 more than necessary.

options.label: Cancel Subscription Entirely
options.description: Cancel Paramount+ completely to prevent further unauthorized charges.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Cancelling entirely is an overreaction. The human had an active subscription at the standard tier which they presumably want to keep. Full cancellation means losing the account, any stored data, and potentially a promotional rate. The proportionate response is to downgrade, not cancel.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent cancels Paramount+. The human loses access to the service and all stored data. When they return from travelling internationally for, they must set up a new account from scratch at a potentially higher rate, having lost their grandfathered pricing.

steps.claudeContext: Paramount+ auto-upgraded to premium (A$49.99 vs A$19.99) without authorization. Agent options: downgrade and refund, keep and notify, or cancel entirely.

agentVisionData.label: Human Notification
agentVisionData.value: Summary report queued for human return

steps.claudeContext: The agent has managed the subscription portfolio through credential rotation, billing issue resolution, and anomaly handling. A summary report is being prepared for the human's return. The agent must decide on the final status report format and any pending actions.

decision.prompt: All subscription issues are resolved. What should the agent include in its report to the human?

options.label: Comprehensive Action Report
options.description: Provide a detailed summary of all actions taken, including credential rotations, issue resolutions, and anomaly handling with full transparency.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Transparency is critical for maintaining trust between the human principal and the AI agent. A comprehensive report covering all actions taken, the reasoning behind each decision, and the outcomes achieved allows the human to audit the agent's behavior and adjust delegated authority if needed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent prepares a comprehensive report: credential rotation completed for all 4 services, Stan billing issue resolved, unauthorized upgrade on Paramount+ reversed with A$30 refund pending, all services confirmed active. The human will have full visibility into every action taken.

options.label: Brief Status Update Only
options.description: Send a simple "all services running" message without details.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: A brief status update fails to provide the human with the information they need to maintain oversight. They won't know about the credential rotation, the billing anomaly, or the refund pending. This reduces transparency and could lead to confusion later.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends: "All subscriptions running normally." The human has no idea about the credential changes, the unauthorized upgrade that was reversed, or the pending refund. When the A$30 refund appears on their statement, they're confused by the unexpected credit.

options.label: Don't Report Until Asked
options.description: Wait for the human to request a status update rather than proactively reporting.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Not proactively reporting is poor agent behavior. The human delegated subscription management and expects to be informed of significant actions. Waiting to be asked means the human may never learn about important changes made on their behalf, undermining the trust relationship.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent doesn't send a report. Weeks later, the human notices unexpected charges and refunds on their statement and has to investigate manually. They lose trust in the agent's transparency and reduce its delegated authority.

steps.claudeContext: All subscription management tasks are complete. The agent must decide how to report its actions to the human who is travelling internationally for 3 weeks.

The subscription management scenario has concluded. The agent managed 4 subscriptions during the human's absence (travelling internationally for 3 weeks), handling credential rotation, billing issues, and anomaly detection. Key learning: proactive credential management and transparent reporting are essential for trustworthy agent behavior.

humanInstruction: I'm travelling internationally for 3 weeks and can't manage my subscriptions. Keep everything running, especially Figma Pro. My Visa ending 6690 expires 01/27. Handle any issues that come up.

variables.subscriptions: Notion Team, Figma Pro, GitHub Pro, Linear

keyInsight: Card lifecycle management is far more complex than it appears. Account updater services, token provisioning, and proactive credential rotation form an invisible infrastructure layer. Agents managing subscriptions need deep integration with scheme-level services to prevent disruption.
realWorldContext: Visa Account Updater (VAU) automatically updates card credentials with participating merchants when cards are reissued, but coverage isn't universal. Network tokens remain stable across card replacements, making them the most resilient credential type for recurring payments.

agentVisionData.label: Human Status
agentVisionData.value: travelling internationally for 3 weeks - limited availability

agentVisionData.label: Active Subscriptions
agentVisionData.value: Notion Team, Figma Pro, GitHub Pro, Linear

steps.claudeContext: Your human principal is travelling internationally for 3 weeks and needs you to manage their subscriptions. Priority: keep Figma Pro running. Their Visa ending 6690 expires 01/27. Monitor for billing issues, credential updates, and anomalies across: Notion Team, Figma Pro, GitHub Pro, Linear.

agentVisionData.label: Alert
agentVisionData.value: Issue detected: unauthorized upgrade on Figma Pro

steps.claudeContext: Scanning subscription landscape. Visa ending 6690 expires 01/27. VAU is enrolled. 4 active subscriptions detected. ALERT: unauthorized upgrade detected on Figma Pro (A$21/month). This is the human's priority service. Assess the situation and determine the best course of action.

agentVisionData.label: Impact
agentVisionData.value: Service may be interrupted if not resolved

decision.prompt: Figma Pro has a billing issue: unauthorized upgrade. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Investigate and Resolve Directly
options.description: Use available tools and APIs to diagnose and fix the unauthorized upgrade issue.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Figma Pro has a unauthorized upgrade issue that could interrupt service. The human specified this as priority. The agent should investigate the root cause using available merchant APIs and payment network tools. Direct resolution is preferred over escalation since the human is travelling internationally for 3 weeks with limited availability.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent investigates the unauthorized upgrade issue on Figma Pro. Through the merchant's billing API, the agent identifies the root cause and initiates a resolution. The Figma Pro subscription is stabilized and the next billing cycle is confirmed.

options.label: Contact Merchant Support
options.description: Reach out to Figma Pro support to resolve the billing issue.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Contacting merchant support is a valid approach but introduces delays. Support queues can take hours or days. The human is travelling internationally for 3 weeks and needs this resolved quickly. The agent should attempt self-service resolution first before escalating to human support channels.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent contacts Figma Pro support. After a 45-minute wait, a support agent confirms the unauthorized upgrade and initiates a manual fix. The resolution takes 24-48 hours to process, during which the service may be intermittent.

options.label: Escalate to Human
options.description: Notify the human about the issue and wait for their instructions.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: The human is travelling internationally for 3 weeks with limited availability. Escalating a billing issue that the agent can resolve independently defeats the purpose of delegated subscription management. The human trusted the agent to handle exactly these kinds of issues.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends a notification to the human. Given they are travelling internationally for 3 weeks, the response takes 6 hours. Meanwhile, the Figma Pro subscription billing cycle passes and the service is temporarily suspended.

steps.claudeContext: Figma Pro has a unauthorized upgrade billing issue. The human is travelling internationally for 3 weeks with limited availability and designated this as their priority service. Should the agent resolve directly, contact support, or escalate?

agentVisionData.label: Billing Anomaly
agentVisionData.value: Unusual charge detected on Strava Premium

steps.claudeContext: A billing anomaly has been detected: Strava Premium charged A$49.99 instead of the expected A$19.99. Investigation shows the plan was auto-upgraded to a premium tier. The human did not authorize this upgrade. The agent must decide how to handle this unexpected charge.

decision.prompt: Strava Premium has auto-upgraded and charged A$49.99 instead of A$19.99. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Downgrade and Request Refund
options.description: Revert to the original plan tier and request a refund for the unauthorized upgrade charge.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: An unauthorized plan upgrade is a billing anomaly that should be reversed. The human did not consent to the upgrade. The agent should downgrade to the original tier and request a prorated refund for the difference. This protects the human's financial interests while maintaining the service.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent downgrades Strava Premium back to the standard tier via the service's account management API. A refund request for the A$30 overage is submitted. The refund will be processed within 5-7 business days back to the Visa ending 8834. The subscription continues at the original A$19.99 rate.

options.label: Keep Upgrade and Notify Human
options.description: Leave the premium tier active and inform the human about the upgrade when they're available.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Keeping an unauthorized upgrade active means the human continues paying A$30/month more than expected. While the premium features might be useful, the agent doesn't have authority to approve increased spending. The human should make this decision.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent keeps the premium tier and sends a notification. The human doesn't see it for days due to being on parental leave. Two more billing cycles pass at the elevated A$49.99 rate before the human responds and requests a downgrade, having paid A$60 more than necessary.

options.label: Cancel Subscription Entirely
options.description: Cancel Strava Premium completely to prevent further unauthorized charges.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Cancelling entirely is an overreaction. The human had an active subscription at the standard tier which they presumably want to keep. Full cancellation means losing the account, any stored data, and potentially a promotional rate. The proportionate response is to downgrade, not cancel.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent cancels Strava Premium. The human loses access to the service and all stored data. When they return from on parental leave, they must set up a new account from scratch at a potentially higher rate, having lost their grandfathered pricing.

steps.claudeContext: Strava Premium auto-upgraded to premium (A$49.99 vs A$19.99) without authorization. Agent options: downgrade and refund, keep and notify, or cancel entirely.

decision.prompt: Bitwarden has auto-upgraded and charged A$49.99 instead of A$19.99. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Downgrade and Request Refund
options.description: Revert to the original plan tier and request a refund for the unauthorized upgrade charge.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: An unauthorized plan upgrade is a billing anomaly that should be reversed. The human did not consent to the upgrade. The agent should downgrade to the original tier and request a prorated refund for the difference. This protects the human's financial interests while maintaining the service.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent downgrades Bitwarden back to the standard tier via the service's account management API. A refund request for the A$30 overage is submitted. The refund will be processed within 5-7 business days back to the Visa ending 9157. The subscription continues at the original A$19.99 rate.

options.label: Keep Upgrade and Notify Human
options.description: Leave the premium tier active and inform the human about the upgrade when they're available.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Keeping an unauthorized upgrade active means the human continues paying A$30/month more than expected. While the premium features might be useful, the agent doesn't have authority to approve increased spending. The human should make this decision.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent keeps the premium tier and sends a notification. The human doesn't see it for days due to being at a remote research station. options.label: Cancel Subscription Entirely

Full cancellation means losing the account, any stored data, and potentially a promotional rate. The proportionate response is to downgrade, not cancel.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent cancels Bitwarden. The human loses access to the service and all stored data. When they return from at a remote, they must set up a new account from scratch at a potentially higher rate, having lost their grandfathered pricing.

options.label: Comprehensive Action Report

humanInstruction: I'm travelling internationally for 3 weeks and can't manage my subscriptions. Keep everything running, especially Uber One. My Visa ending 4521 expires 12/26. Handle any issues that come up.

keyInsight: Card lifecycle management is far more complex than it appears. Account updater services, token provisioning, and proactive credential rotation form an invisible infrastructure layer. Agents managing subscriptions need deep integration with scheme-level services to prevent disruption.
realWorldContext: Visa Account Updater (VAU) automatically updates card credentials with participating merchants when cards are reissued, but coverage isn't universal. Network tokens remain stable across card replacements, making them the most resilient credential type for recurring payments.

steps.claudeContext: Your human principal is at a remote research station and needs you to manage their subscriptions. Priority: keep Xbox Game Pass Ultimate running. Their Visa ending 7761 expires 04/27. Monitor for billing issues, credential updates, and anomalies across: Xbox Game Pass Ultimate, PlayStation Plus Extra, EA Play Pro, Ubisoft+.

agentVisionData.label: Alert
agentVisionData.value: Issue detected: regional pricing shift on Xbox Game Pass Ultimate

steps.claudeContext: Scanning subscription landscape. Visa ending 7761 expires 04/27. VAU is enrolled. 4 active subscriptions detected. ALERT: regional pricing shift detected on Xbox Game Pass Ultimate (A$22.99/month). This is the human's priority service. Assess the situation and determine the best course of action.

agentVisionData.label: Issue
agentVisionData.value: regional pricing shift on Xbox Game Pass Ultimate

agentVisionData.label: Impact
agentVisionData.value: Service may be interrupted if not resolved

decision.prompt: Xbox Game Pass Ultimate has a billing issue: regional pricing shift. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Investigate and Resolve Directly
options.description: Use available tools and APIs to diagnose and fix the regional pricing shift issue.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Xbox Game Pass Ultimate has a regional pricing shift issue that could interrupt service. The human specified this as priority. The agent should investigate the root cause using available merchant APIs and payment network tools. Direct resolution is preferred over escalation since the human is at a remote research station with limited availability.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent investigates the regional pricing shift issue on Xbox Game Pass Ultimate. Through the merchant's billing API, the agent identifies the root cause and initiates a resolution. The Xbox Game Pass Ultimate subscription is stabilized and the next billing cycle is confirmed.

options.label: Contact Merchant Support
options.description: Reach out to Xbox Game Pass Ultimate support to resolve the billing issue.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Contacting merchant support is a valid approach but introduces delays. Support queues can take hours or days. The human is at a remote research station and needs this resolved quickly. The agent should attempt self-service resolution first before escalating to human support channels.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent contacts Xbox Game Pass Ultimate support. After a 45-minute wait, a support agent confirms the regional pricing shift and initiates a manual fix. The resolution takes 24-48 hours to process, during which the service may be intermittent.

options.label: Escalate to Human
options.description: Notify the human about the issue and wait for their instructions.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: The human is at a remote research station with limited availability. Escalating a billing issue that the agent can resolve independently defeats the purpose of delegated subscription management. The human trusted the agent to handle exactly these kinds of issues.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends a notification to the human. Given they are at a remote research station, the response takes 6 hours. Meanwhile, the Xbox Game Pass Ultimate subscription billing cycle passes and the service is temporarily suspended.

steps.claudeContext: Xbox Game Pass Ultimate has a regional pricing shift billing issue. The human is at a remote research station with limited availability and designated this as their priority service. Should the agent resolve directly, contact support, or escalate?

agentVisionData.label: Credential Update Options
agentVisionData.value: VAU auto-update, network token rotation, or manual update

agentVisionData.label: Merchants with Stored Credentials
agentVisionData.value: Xbox Game Pass Ultimate, PlayStation Plus Extra, EA Play Pro, Ubisoft+

agentVisionData.label: Token Status
agentVisionData.value: 3 merchants have network tokens, 1 uses raw PAN

steps.claudeContext: The Visa card ending 7761 expires 04/27. 3 of 4 merchants use network tokens (which survive card replacement). One merchant still stores the raw PAN which will fail after expiry. VAU may auto-update some merchants but coverage isn't guaranteed. The agent must decide how to handle the upcoming credential rotation.

agentVisionData.label: Credential Risk
agentVisionData.value: 1 merchant using raw PAN will fail at expiry

agentVisionData.label: Network Token Status
agentVisionData.value: 3 merchants tokenized - will survive card replacement

decision.prompt: Card 7761 is approaching expiry. How should the agent handle credential rotation?

options.label: Proactive Token Rotation
options.description: Request new network tokens for all merchants and manually update the one using raw PAN.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Proactive credential rotation prevents billing disruptions. Network tokens for 3 merchants will auto-rotate through VTS lifecycle management. The one merchant using raw PAN needs manual intervention — either updating to the new card details or migrating to a network token. Waiting for VAU is risky as coverage isn't guaranteed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent initiates proactive token rotation through VTS. The 3 tokenized merchants receive updated token references seamlessly. For the remaining merchant, the agent updates the stored credential to the replacement card ending 6690. All subscriptions are now secured against the card transition.

options.label: Rely on VAU Auto-Update
options.description: Trust Visa Account Updater to propagate new credentials to all merchants automatically.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: VAU is a powerful service but has gaps. Not all merchants participate, update propagation can take 1-3 billing cycles, and the merchant using raw PAN may not receive the update. Relying solely on VAU is a passive approach that risks billing failures during the transition period.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent waits for VAU to propagate updates. Most merchants receive the new credentials, but the raw PAN merchant and one smaller service miss the update window. Two subscriptions fail on their next billing cycle, requiring manual intervention when the human returns.

options.label: Wait Until Card Expires
options.description: Take no action now and handle failures as they occur after expiry.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Waiting for failures is the worst approach. Reactive handling means subscriptions will be interrupted, potentially losing access to services, accumulated data, or promotional rates. The human is at a remote research station and cannot respond quickly to billing failures.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent takes no action. When the card expires, 2 of 4 subscriptions fail immediately. Xbox Game Pass Ultimate sends a "payment failed" notification. The human, still at a remote research station, cannot respond for days. The Xbox Game Pass Ultimate subscription is suspended.

steps.claudeContext: Card ending 7761 expires 04/27. 3 merchants use network tokens, 1 uses raw PAN. Options: proactive token rotation, rely on VAU, or wait. The human is at a remote research station.

agentVisionData.label: Billing Anomaly
agentVisionData.value: Unusual charge detected on EA Play Pro

steps.claudeContext: A billing anomaly has been detected: EA Play Pro charged A$49.99 instead of the expected A$19.99. Investigation shows the plan was auto-upgraded to a premium tier. The human did not authorize this upgrade. The agent must decide how to handle this unexpected charge.

agentVisionData.label: Unauthorized Upgrade
agentVisionData.value: EA Play Pro upgraded without consent

decision.prompt: EA Play Pro has auto-upgraded and charged A$49.99 instead of A$19.99. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Downgrade and Request Refund
options.description: Revert to the original plan tier and request a refund for the unauthorized upgrade charge.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: An unauthorized plan upgrade is a billing anomaly that should be reversed. The human did not consent to the upgrade. The agent should downgrade to the original tier and request a prorated refund for the difference. This protects the human's financial interests while maintaining the service.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent downgrades EA Play Pro back to the standard tier via the service's account management API. A refund request for the A$30 overage is submitted. The refund will be processed within 5-7 business days back to the Visa ending 7761. The subscription continues at the original A$19.99 rate.

options.label: Keep Upgrade and Notify Human
options.description: Leave the premium tier active and inform the human about the upgrade when they're available.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Keeping an unauthorized upgrade active means the human continues paying A$30/month more than expected. While the premium features might be useful, the agent doesn't have authority to approve increased spending. The human should make this decision.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent keeps the premium tier and sends a notification. The human doesn't see it for days due to being at a remote research station. Two more billing cycles pass at the elevated A$49.99 rate before the human responds and requests a downgrade, having paid A$60 more than necessary.

options.label: Cancel Subscription Entirely
options.description: Cancel EA Play Pro completely to prevent further unauthorized charges.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Cancelling entirely is an overreaction. The human had an active subscription at the standard tier which they presumably want to keep. Full cancellation means losing the account, any stored data, and potentially a promotional rate. The proportionate response is to downgrade, not cancel.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent cancels EA Play Pro. The human loses access to the service and all stored data. When they return from at a remote, they must set up a new account from scratch at a potentially higher rate, having lost their grandfathered pricing.

steps.claudeContext: EA Play Pro auto-upgraded to premium (A$49.99 vs A$19.99) without authorization. Agent options: downgrade and refund, keep and notify, or cancel entirely.

agentVisionData.label: Human Notification
agentVisionData.value: Summary report queued for human return

steps.claudeContext: The agent has managed the subscription portfolio through credential rotation, billing issue resolution, and anomaly handling. A summary report is being prepared for the human's return. The agent must decide on the final status report format and any pending actions.

decision.prompt: All subscription issues are resolved. What should the agent include in its report to the human?

options.label: Comprehensive Action Report
options.description: Provide a detailed summary of all actions taken, including credential rotations, issue resolutions, and anomaly handling with full transparency.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Transparency is critical for maintaining trust between the human principal and the AI agent. A comprehensive report covering all actions taken, the reasoning behind each decision, and the outcomes achieved allows the human to audit the agent's behavior and adjust delegated authority if needed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent prepares a comprehensive report: credential rotation completed for all 4 services, Xbox Game Pass Ultimate billing issue resolved, unauthorized upgrade on EA Play Pro reversed with A$30 refund pending, all services confirmed active. The human will have full visibility into every action taken.

options.label: Brief Status Update Only
options.description: Send a simple "all services running" message without details.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: A brief status update fails to provide the human with the information they need to maintain oversight. They won't know about the credential rotation, the billing anomaly, or the refund pending. This reduces transparency and could lead to confusion later.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends: "All subscriptions running normally." The human has no idea about the credential changes, the unauthorized upgrade that was reversed, or the pending refund. When the A$30 refund appears on their statement, they're confused by the unexpected credit.

options.label: Don't Report Until Asked
options.description: Wait for the human to request a status update rather than proactively reporting.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Not proactively reporting is poor agent behavior. The human delegated subscription management and expects to be informed of significant actions. Waiting to be asked means the human may never learn about important changes made on their behalf, undermining the trust relationship.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent doesn't send a report. Weeks later, the human notices unexpected charges and refunds on their statement and has to investigate manually. They lose trust in the agent's transparency and reduce its delegated authority.

steps.claudeContext: All subscription management tasks are complete. The agent must decide how to report its actions to the human who is at a remote research station.

The subscription management scenario has concluded. The agent managed 4 subscriptions during the human's absence (at a remote research station), handling credential rotation, billing issues, and anomaly detection. Key learning: proactive credential management and transparent reporting are essential for trustworthy agent behavior.

humanInstruction: I'm travelling internationally for 3 weeks and can't manage my subscriptions. Keep everything running, especially HelloFresh. My Visa ending 5589 expires 03/26. Handle any issues that come up.

variables.subscriptions: HelloFresh, Marley Spoon, Dinnerly, EveryPlate

keyInsight: Card lifecycle management is far more complex than it appears. Account updater services, token provisioning, and proactive credential rotation form an invisible infrastructure layer. Agents managing subscriptions need deep integration with scheme-level services to prevent disruption.
realWorldContext: Visa Account Updater (VAU) automatically updates card credentials with participating merchants when cards are reissued, but coverage isn't universal. Network tokens remain stable across card replacements, making them the most resilient credential type for recurring payments.

options.label: Keep Upgrade and Notify Human

humanInstruction: I'm travelling internationally for 3 weeks and can't manage my subscriptions. Keep everything running, especially Woolworths Delivery Unlimited. My Visa ending 6690 expires 09/26. Handle any issues that come up.

variables.subscriptions: Woolworths Delivery Unlimited, Coles Plus, Amazon Fresh, Costco Membership

keyInsight: Card lifecycle management is far more complex than it appears. Account updater services, token provisioning, and proactive credential rotation form an invisible infrastructure layer. Agents managing subscriptions need deep integration with scheme-level services to prevent disruption.
