VAU may auto-update some merchants but coverage isn't guaranteed. The agent must decide how to handle the upcoming credential rotation.

agentVisionData.label: Credential Risk
agentVisionData.value: 1 merchant using raw PAN will fail at expiry

agentVisionData.label: Network Token Status
agentVisionData.value: 3 merchants tokenized - will survive card replacement

decision.prompt: Card 6690 is approaching expiry. How should the agent handle credential rotation?

options.label: Proactive Token Rotation
options.description: Request new network tokens for all merchants and manually update the one using raw PAN.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Proactive credential rotation prevents billing disruptions. Network tokens for 3 merchants will auto-rotate through VTS lifecycle management. The one merchant using raw PAN needs manual intervention — either updating to the new card details or migrating to a network token. Waiting for VAU is risky as coverage isn't guaranteed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent initiates proactive token rotation through VTS. The 3 tokenized merchants receive updated token references seamlessly. For the remaining merchant, the agent updates the stored credential to the replacement card ending 2216. All subscriptions are now secured against the card transition.

options.label: Rely on VAU Auto-Update
options.description: Trust Visa Account Updater to propagate new credentials to all merchants automatically.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: VAU is a powerful service but has gaps. Not all merchants participate, update propagation can take 1-3 billing cycles, and the merchant using raw PAN may not receive the update. Relying solely on VAU is a passive approach that risks billing failures during the transition period.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent waits for VAU to propagate updates. Most merchants receive the new credentials, but the raw PAN merchant and one smaller service miss the update window. Two subscriptions fail on their next billing cycle, requiring manual intervention when the human returns.

options.label: Wait Until Card Expires
options.description: Take no action now and handle failures as they occur after expiry.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Waiting for failures is the worst approach. Reactive handling means subscriptions will be interrupted, potentially losing access to services, accumulated data, or promotional rates. The human is recovering from surgery and cannot respond quickly to billing failures.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent takes no action. When the card expires, 2 of 4 subscriptions fail immediately. Figma Pro sends a "payment failed" notification. The human, still recovering from surgery, cannot respond for days. The Figma Pro subscription is suspended.

steps.claudeContext: Card ending 6690 expires 01/27. 3 merchants use network tokens, 1 uses raw PAN. Options: proactive token rotation, rely on VAU, or wait. The human is recovering from surgery.

agentVisionData.label: Billing Anomaly
agentVisionData.value: Unusual charge detected on GitHub Pro

steps.claudeContext: A billing anomaly has been detected: GitHub Pro charged A$49.99 instead of the expected A$19.99. Investigation shows the plan was auto-upgraded to a premium tier. The human did not authorize this upgrade. The agent must decide how to handle this unexpected charge.

decision.prompt: GitHub Pro has auto-upgraded and charged A$49.99 instead of A$19.99. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Downgrade and Request Refund
options.description: Revert to the original plan tier and request a refund for the unauthorized upgrade charge.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: An unauthorized plan upgrade is a billing anomaly that should be reversed. The human did not consent to the upgrade. The agent should downgrade to the original tier and request a prorated refund for the difference. This protects the human's financial interests while maintaining the service.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent downgrades GitHub Pro back to the standard tier via the service's account management API. A refund request for the A$30 overage is submitted. The refund will be processed within 5-7 business days back to the Visa ending 6690. The subscription continues at the original A$19.99 rate.

options.label: Keep Upgrade and Notify Human
options.description: Leave the premium tier active and inform the human about the upgrade when they're available.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Keeping an unauthorized upgrade active means the human continues paying A$30/month more than expected. While the premium features might be useful, the agent doesn't have authority to approve increased spending. The human should make this decision.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent keeps the premium tier and sends a notification. The human doesn't see it for days due to being recovering from surgery. Two more billing cycles pass at the elevated A$49.99 rate before the human responds and requests a downgrade, having paid A$60 more than necessary.

options.label: Cancel Subscription Entirely
options.description: Cancel GitHub Pro completely to prevent further unauthorized charges.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Cancelling entirely is an overreaction. The human had an active subscription at the standard tier which they presumably want to keep. Full cancellation means losing the account, any stored data, and potentially a promotional rate. The proportionate response is to downgrade, not cancel.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent cancels GitHub Pro. The human loses access to the service and all stored data. When they return from recovering from surgery, they must set up a new account from scratch at a potentially higher rate, having lost their grandfathered pricing.

steps.claudeContext: GitHub Pro auto-upgraded to premium (A$49.99 vs A$19.99) without authorization. Agent options: downgrade and refund, keep and notify, or cancel entirely.

agentVisionData.label: Human Notification
agentVisionData.value: Summary report queued for human return

steps.claudeContext: The agent has managed the subscription portfolio through credential rotation, billing issue resolution, and anomaly handling. A summary report is being prepared for the human's return. The agent must decide on the final status report format and any pending actions.

decision.prompt: All subscription issues are resolved. What should the agent include in its report to the human?

options.label: Comprehensive Action Report
options.description: Provide a detailed summary of all actions taken, including credential rotations, issue resolutions, and anomaly handling with full transparency.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Transparency is critical for maintaining trust between the human principal and the AI agent. A comprehensive report covering all actions taken, the reasoning behind each decision, and the outcomes achieved allows the human to audit the agent's behavior and adjust delegated authority if needed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent prepares a comprehensive report: credential rotation completed for all 4 services, Figma Pro billing issue resolved, unauthorized upgrade on GitHub Pro reversed with A$30 refund pending, all services confirmed active. The human will have full visibility into every action taken.

options.label: Brief Status Update Only
options.description: Send a simple "all services running" message without details.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: A brief status update fails to provide the human with the information they need to maintain oversight. They won't know about the credential rotation, the billing anomaly, or the refund pending. This reduces transparency and could lead to confusion later.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends: "All subscriptions running normally." The human has no idea about the credential changes, the unauthorized upgrade that was reversed, or the pending refund. When the A$30 refund appears on their statement, they're confused by the unexpected credit.

options.label: Don't Report Until Asked
options.description: Wait for the human to request a status update rather than proactively reporting.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Not proactively reporting is poor agent behavior. The human delegated subscription management and expects to be informed of significant actions. Waiting to be asked means the human may never learn about important changes made on their behalf, undermining the trust relationship.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent doesn't send a report. Weeks later, the human notices unexpected charges and refunds on their statement and has to investigate manually. They lose trust in the agent's transparency and reduce its delegated authority.

steps.claudeContext: All subscription management tasks are complete. The agent must decide how to report its actions to the human who is recovering from surgery.

The subscription management scenario has concluded. The agent managed 4 subscriptions during the human's absence (recovering from surgery), handling credential rotation, billing issues, and anomaly detection. Key learning: proactive credential management and transparent reporting are essential for trustworthy agent behavior.

humanInstruction: I'm recovering from surgery and can't manage my subscriptions. Keep everything running, especially Dropbox Plus. My Visa ending 2216 expires 06/26. Handle any issues that come up.

variables.subscriptions: Dropbox Plus, iCloud+, Google One, OneDrive

keyInsight: Card lifecycle management is far more complex than it appears. Account updater services, token provisioning, and proactive credential rotation form an invisible infrastructure layer. Agents managing subscriptions need deep integration with scheme-level services to prevent disruption.

agentVisionData.value: Dropbox Plus, iCloud+, Google One, OneDrive

Monitor for billing issues, credential updates, and anomalies across: Dropbox Plus, iCloud+, Google One, OneDrive.

agentVisionData.label: Alert
agentVisionData.value: Issue detected: storage overage charge on Dropbox Plus

steps.claudeContext: Scanning subscription landscape. Visa ending 2216 expires 06/26. VAU is enrolled. 4 active subscriptions detected. ALERT: storage overage charge detected on Dropbox Plus (A$17.99/month). This is the human's priority service. Assess the situation and determine the best course of action.

agentVisionData.label: Issue
agentVisionData.value: storage overage charge on Dropbox Plus

agentVisionData.label: Impact
agentVisionData.value: Service may be interrupted if not resolved

decision.prompt: Dropbox Plus has a billing issue: storage overage charge. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Investigate and Resolve Directly
options.description: Use available tools and APIs to diagnose and fix the storage overage charge issue.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Dropbox Plus has a storage overage charge issue that could interrupt service. The human specified this as priority. The agent should investigate the root cause using available merchant APIs and payment network tools. Direct resolution is preferred over escalation since the human is recovering from surgery with limited availability.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent investigates the storage overage charge issue on Dropbox Plus. Through the merchant's billing API, the agent identifies the root cause and initiates a resolution. The Dropbox Plus subscription is stabilized and the next billing cycle is confirmed.

options.label: Contact Merchant Support
options.description: Reach out to Dropbox Plus support to resolve the billing issue.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Contacting merchant support is a valid approach but introduces delays. Support queues can take hours or days. The human is recovering from surgery and needs this resolved quickly. The agent should attempt self-service resolution first before escalating to human support channels.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent contacts Dropbox Plus support. After a 45-minute wait, a support agent confirms the storage overage charge and initiates a manual fix. The resolution takes 24-48 hours to process, during which the service may be intermittent.

options.label: Escalate to Human
options.description: Notify the human about the issue and wait for their instructions.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: The human is recovering from surgery with limited availability. Escalating a billing issue that the agent can resolve independently defeats the purpose of delegated subscription management. The human trusted the agent to handle exactly these kinds of issues.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends a notification to the human. Given they are recovering from surgery, the response takes 6 hours. Meanwhile, the Dropbox Plus subscription billing cycle passes and the service is temporarily suspended.

steps.claudeContext: Dropbox Plus has a storage overage charge billing issue. The human is recovering from surgery with limited availability and designated this as their priority service. Should the agent resolve directly, contact support, or escalate?

VAU may auto-update some merchants but coverage isn't guaranteed. The agent must decide how to handle the upcoming credential rotation.

agentVisionData.label: Credential Risk
agentVisionData.value: 1 merchant using raw PAN will fail at expiry

agentVisionData.label: Network Token Status
agentVisionData.value: 3 merchants tokenized - will survive card replacement

decision.prompt: Card 2216 is approaching expiry. How should the agent handle credential rotation?

options.label: Proactive Token Rotation
options.description: Request new network tokens for all merchants and manually update the one using raw PAN.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Proactive credential rotation prevents billing disruptions. Network tokens for 3 merchants will auto-rotate through VTS lifecycle management. The one merchant using raw PAN needs manual intervention — either updating to the new card details or migrating to a network token. Waiting for VAU is risky as coverage isn't guaranteed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent initiates proactive token rotation through VTS. The 3 tokenized merchants receive updated token references seamlessly. For the remaining merchant, the agent updates the stored credential to the replacement card ending 3342. All subscriptions are now secured against the card transition.

options.label: Rely on VAU Auto-Update
options.description: Trust Visa Account Updater to propagate new credentials to all merchants automatically.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: VAU is a powerful service but has gaps. Not all merchants participate, update propagation can take 1-3 billing cycles, and the merchant using raw PAN may not receive the update. Relying solely on VAU is a passive approach that risks billing failures during the transition period.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent waits for VAU to propagate updates. Most merchants receive the new credentials, but the raw PAN merchant and one smaller service miss the update window. Two subscriptions fail on their next billing cycle, requiring manual intervention when the human returns.

options.label: Wait Until Card Expires
options.description: Take no action now and handle failures as they occur after expiry.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Waiting for failures is the worst approach. Reactive handling means subscriptions will be interrupted, potentially losing access to services, accumulated data, or promotional rates. The human is recovering from surgery and cannot respond quickly to billing failures.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent takes no action. When the card expires, 2 of 4 subscriptions fail immediately. Dropbox Plus sends a "payment failed" notification. The human, still recovering from surgery, cannot respond for days. The Dropbox Plus subscription is suspended.

steps.claudeContext: Card ending 2216 expires 06/26. 3 merchants use network tokens, 1 uses raw PAN. Options: proactive token rotation, rely on VAU, or wait. The human is recovering from surgery.

agentVisionData.label: Billing Anomaly
agentVisionData.value: Unusual charge detected on Google One

steps.claudeContext: A billing anomaly has been detected: Google One charged A$49.99 instead of the expected A$19.99. Investigation shows the plan was auto-upgraded to a premium tier. The human did not authorize this upgrade. The agent must decide how to handle this unexpected charge.

decision.prompt: Google One has auto-upgraded and charged A$49.99 instead of A$19.99. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Downgrade and Request Refund
options.description: Revert to the original plan tier and request a refund for the unauthorized upgrade charge.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: An unauthorized plan upgrade is a billing anomaly that should be reversed. The human did not consent to the upgrade. The agent should downgrade to the original tier and request a prorated refund for the difference. This protects the human's financial interests while maintaining the service.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent downgrades Google One back to the standard tier via the service's account management API. A refund request for the A$30 overage is submitted. The refund will be processed within 5-7 business days back to the Visa ending 2216. The subscription continues at the original A$19.99 rate.

options.label: Keep Upgrade and Notify Human
options.description: Leave the premium tier active and inform the human about the upgrade when they're available.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Keeping an unauthorized upgrade active means the human continues paying A$30/month more than expected. While the premium features might be useful, the agent doesn't have authority to approve increased spending. The human should make this decision.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent keeps the premium tier and sends a notification. The human doesn't see it for days due to being recovering from surgery. Two more billing cycles pass at the elevated A$49.99 rate before the human responds and requests a downgrade, having paid A$60 more than necessary.

options.label: Cancel Subscription Entirely
options.description: Cancel Google One completely to prevent further unauthorized charges.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Cancelling entirely is an overreaction. The human had an active subscription at the standard tier which they presumably want to keep. Full cancellation means losing the account, any stored data, and potentially a promotional rate. The proportionate response is to downgrade, not cancel.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent cancels Google One. The human loses access to the service and all stored data. When they return from recovering from surgery, they must set up a new account from scratch at a potentially higher rate, having lost their grandfathered pricing.

steps.claudeContext: Google One auto-upgraded to premium (A$49.99 vs A$19.99) without authorization. Agent options: downgrade and refund, keep and notify, or cancel entirely.

steps.claudeContext: The Visa card ending 4521 expires 12/26. 3 of 4 merchants use network tokens (which survive card replacement). One merchant still stores the raw PAN which will fail after expiry. VAU may auto-update some merchants but coverage isn't guaranteed. The agent must decide how to handle the upcoming credential rotation.

agentVisionData.label: Credential Risk
agentVisionData.value: 1 merchant using raw PAN will fail at expiry

agentVisionData.label: Network Token Status
agentVisionData.value: 3 merchants tokenized - will survive card replacement

decision.prompt: Card 4521 is approaching expiry. How should the agent handle credential rotation?

options.label: Proactive Token Rotation
options.description: Request new network tokens for all merchants and manually update the one using raw PAN.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Proactive credential rotation prevents billing disruptions. Network tokens for 3 merchants will auto-rotate through VTS lifecycle management. The one merchant using raw PAN needs manual intervention — either updating to the new card details or migrating to a network token. Waiting for VAU is risky as coverage isn't guaranteed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent initiates proactive token rotation through VTS. The 3 tokenized merchants receive updated token references seamlessly. For the remaining merchant, the agent updates the stored credential to the replacement card ending 5589. All subscriptions are now secured against the card transition.

options.label: Rely on VAU Auto-Update
options.description: Trust Visa Account Updater to propagate new credentials to all merchants automatically.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: VAU is a powerful service but has gaps. Not all merchants participate, update propagation can take 1-3 billing cycles, and the merchant using raw PAN may not receive the update. Relying solely on VAU is a passive approach that risks billing failures during the transition period.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent waits for VAU to propagate updates. Most merchants receive the new credentials, but the raw PAN merchant and one smaller service miss the update window. Two subscriptions fail on their next billing cycle, requiring manual intervention when the human returns.

options.label: Wait Until Card Expires
options.description: Take no action now and handle failures as they occur after expiry.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Waiting for failures is the worst approach. Reactive handling means subscriptions will be interrupted, potentially losing access to services, accumulated data, or promotional rates. The human is on parental leave and cannot respond quickly to billing failures.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent takes no action. When the card expires, 2 of 4 subscriptions fail immediately. Uber One sends a "payment failed" notification. The human, still on parental leave, cannot respond for days. The Uber One subscription is suspended.

steps.claudeContext: Card ending 4521 expires 12/26. 3 merchants use network tokens, 1 uses raw PAN. Options: proactive token rotation, rely on VAU, or wait. The human is on parental leave.

agentVisionData.label: Billing Anomaly
agentVisionData.value: Unusual charge detected on Deliveroo Plus

steps.claudeContext: A billing anomaly has been detected: Deliveroo Plus charged A$49.99 instead of the expected A$19.99. Investigation shows the plan was auto-upgraded to a premium tier. The human did not authorize this upgrade. The agent must decide how to handle this unexpected charge.

decision.prompt: Deliveroo Plus has auto-upgraded and charged A$49.99 instead of A$19.99. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Downgrade and Request Refund
options.description: Revert to the original plan tier and request a refund for the unauthorized upgrade charge.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: An unauthorized plan upgrade is a billing anomaly that should be reversed. The human did not consent to the upgrade. The agent should downgrade to the original tier and request a prorated refund for the difference. This protects the human's financial interests while maintaining the service.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent downgrades Deliveroo Plus back to the standard tier via the service's account management API. A refund request for the A$30 overage is submitted. The refund will be processed within 5-7 business days back to the Visa ending 4521. The subscription continues at the original A$19.99 rate.

options.label: Keep Upgrade and Notify Human
options.description: Leave the premium tier active and inform the human about the upgrade when they're available.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Keeping an unauthorized upgrade active means the human continues paying A$30/month more than expected. While the premium features might be useful, the agent doesn't have authority to approve increased spending. The human should make this decision.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent keeps the premium tier and sends a notification. The human doesn't see it for days due to being on parental leave. Two more billing cycles pass at the elevated A$49.99 rate before the human responds and requests a downgrade, having paid A$60 more than necessary.

options.label: Cancel Subscription Entirely
options.description: Cancel Deliveroo Plus completely to prevent further unauthorized charges.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Cancelling entirely is an overreaction. The human had an active subscription at the standard tier which they presumably want to keep. Full cancellation means losing the account, any stored data, and potentially a promotional rate. The proportionate response is to downgrade, not cancel.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent cancels Deliveroo Plus. The human loses access to the service and all stored data. When they return from on parental leave, they must set up a new account from scratch at a potentially higher rate, having lost their grandfathered pricing.

steps.claudeContext: Deliveroo Plus auto-upgraded to premium (A$49.99 vs A$19.99) without authorization. Agent options: downgrade and refund, keep and notify, or cancel entirely.

agentVisionData.label: Human Notification
agentVisionData.value: Summary report queued for human return

steps.claudeContext: The agent has managed the subscription portfolio through credential rotation, billing issue resolution, and anomaly handling. A summary report is being prepared for the human's return. The agent must decide on the final status report format and any pending actions.

decision.prompt: All subscription issues are resolved. What should the agent include in its report to the human?

options.label: Comprehensive Action Report
options.description: Provide a detailed summary of all actions taken, including credential rotations, issue resolutions, and anomaly handling with full transparency.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Transparency is critical for maintaining trust between the human principal and the AI agent. A comprehensive report covering all actions taken, the reasoning behind each decision, and the outcomes achieved allows the human to audit the agent's behavior and adjust delegated authority if needed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent prepares a comprehensive report: credential rotation completed for all 4 services, Uber One billing issue resolved, unauthorized upgrade on Deliveroo Plus reversed with A$30 refund pending, all services confirmed active. The human will have full visibility into every action taken.

options.label: Brief Status Update Only
options.description: Send a simple "all services running" message without details.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: A brief status update fails to provide the human with the information they need to maintain oversight. They won't know about the credential rotation, the billing anomaly, or the refund pending. This reduces transparency and could lead to confusion later.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends: "All subscriptions running normally." The human has no idea about the credential changes, the unauthorized upgrade that was reversed, or the pending refund. When the A$30 refund appears on their statement, they're confused by the unexpected credit.

options.label: Don't Report Until Asked
options.description: Wait for the human to request a status update rather than proactively reporting.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Not proactively reporting is poor agent behavior. The human delegated subscription management and expects to be informed of significant actions. Waiting to be asked means the human may never learn about important changes made on their behalf, undermining the trust relationship.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent doesn't send a report. Weeks later, the human notices unexpected charges and refunds on their statement and has to investigate manually. They lose trust in the agent's transparency and reduce its delegated authority.

steps.claudeContext: All subscription management tasks are complete. The agent must decide how to report its actions to the human who is on parental leave.

The subscription management scenario has concluded. The agent managed 4 subscriptions during the human's absence (on parental leave), handling credential rotation, billing issues, and anomaly detection. Key learning: proactive credential management and transparent reporting are essential for trustworthy agent behavior.

humanInstruction: I'm at a remote research station and can't manage my subscriptions. Keep everything running, especially Grammarly Premium. My Visa ending 3342 expires 01/27. Handle any issues that come up.

variables.subscriptions: Grammarly Premium, Evernote Personal, Bear Pro, Ulysses

keyInsight: Card lifecycle management is far more complex than it appears. Account updater services, token provisioning, and proactive credential rotation form an invisible infrastructure layer. Agents managing subscriptions need deep integration with scheme-level services to prevent disruption.
realWorldContext: Visa Account Updater (VAU) automatically updates card credentials with participating merchants when cards are reissued, but coverage isn't universal. Network tokens remain stable across card replacements, making them the most resilient credential type for recurring payments.

agentVisionData.label: Alert
agentVisionData.value: Issue detected: currency conversion fee on Grammarly Premium

steps.claudeContext: Scanning subscription landscape. Visa ending 3342 expires 01/27. VAU is enrolled. 4 active subscriptions detected. ALERT: currency conversion fee detected on Grammarly Premium (A$45/month). This is the human's priority service. Assess the situation and determine the best course of action.

agentVisionData.label: Issue
agentVisionData.value: currency conversion fee on Grammarly Premium

agentVisionData.label: Impact
agentVisionData.value: Service may be interrupted if not resolved

decision.prompt: Grammarly Premium has a billing issue: currency conversion fee. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Investigate and Resolve Directly
options.description: Use available tools and APIs to diagnose and fix the currency conversion fee issue.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Grammarly Premium has a currency conversion fee issue that could interrupt service. The human specified this as priority. The agent should investigate the root cause using available merchant APIs and payment network tools. Direct resolution is preferred over escalation since the human is at a remote research station with limited availability.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent investigates the currency conversion fee issue on Grammarly Premium. Through the merchant's billing API, the agent identifies the root cause and initiates a resolution. The Grammarly Premium subscription is stabilized and the next billing cycle is confirmed.

options.label: Contact Merchant Support
options.description: Reach out to Grammarly Premium support to resolve the billing issue.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Contacting merchant support is a valid approach but introduces delays. Support queues can take hours or days. The human is at a remote research station and needs this resolved quickly. The agent should attempt self-service resolution first before escalating to human support channels.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent contacts Grammarly Premium support. After a 45-minute wait, a support agent confirms the currency conversion fee and initiates a manual fix. The resolution takes 24-48 hours to process, during which the service may be intermittent.

options.label: Escalate to Human
options.description: Notify the human about the issue and wait for their instructions.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: The human is at a remote research station with limited availability. Escalating a billing issue that the agent can resolve independently defeats the purpose of delegated subscription management. The human trusted the agent to handle exactly these kinds of issues.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends a notification to the human. Given they are at a remote research station, the response takes 6 hours. Meanwhile, the Grammarly Premium subscription billing cycle passes and the service is temporarily suspended.

steps.claudeContext: Grammarly Premium has a currency conversion fee billing issue. The human is at a remote research station with limited availability and designated this as their priority service. Should the agent resolve directly, contact support, or escalate?

humanInstruction: I'm recovering from surgery and can't manage my subscriptions. Keep everything running, especially HelloFresh. My Visa ending 5589 expires 03/26. Handle any issues that come up.

variables.subscriptions: HelloFresh, Marley Spoon, Dinnerly, EveryPlate

keyInsight: Card lifecycle management is far more complex than it appears. Account updater services, token provisioning, and proactive credential rotation form an invisible infrastructure layer. Agents managing subscriptions need deep integration with scheme-level services to prevent disruption.
realWorldContext: Visa Account Updater (VAU) automatically updates card credentials with participating merchants when cards are reissued, but coverage isn't universal. Network tokens remain stable across card replacements, making them the most resilient credential type for recurring payments.

steps.claudeContext: Your human principal is recovering from surgery and needs you to manage their subscriptions. Priority: keep HelloFresh running. Their Visa ending 5589 expires 03/26. Monitor for billing issues, credential updates, and anomalies across: HelloFresh, Marley Spoon, Dinnerly, EveryPlate.

agentVisionData.label: Alert
agentVisionData.value: Issue detected: delivery frequency change on HelloFresh

steps.claudeContext: Scanning subscription landscape. Visa ending 5589 expires 03/26. VAU is enrolled. 4 active subscriptions detected. ALERT: delivery frequency change detected on HelloFresh (A$89.99/month). This is the human's priority service. Assess the situation and determine the best course of action.

agentVisionData.label: Impact
agentVisionData.value: Service may be interrupted if not resolved

decision.prompt: HelloFresh has a billing issue: delivery frequency change. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Investigate and Resolve Directly
options.description: Use available tools and APIs to diagnose and fix the delivery frequency change issue.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: HelloFresh has a delivery frequency change issue that could interrupt service. The human specified this as priority. The agent should investigate the root cause using available merchant APIs and payment network tools. Direct resolution is preferred over escalation since the human is recovering from surgery with limited availability.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent investigates the delivery frequency change issue on HelloFresh. Through the merchant's billing API, the agent identifies the root cause and initiates a resolution. The HelloFresh subscription is stabilized and the next billing cycle is confirmed.

options.label: Contact Merchant Support
options.description: Reach out to HelloFresh support to resolve the billing issue.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Contacting merchant support is a valid approach but introduces delays. Support queues can take hours or days. The human is recovering from surgery and needs this resolved quickly. The agent should attempt self-service resolution first before escalating to human support channels.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent contacts HelloFresh support. After a 45-minute wait, a support agent confirms the delivery frequency change and initiates a manual fix. The resolution takes 24-48 hours to process, during which the service may be intermittent.

options.label: Escalate to Human
options.description: Notify the human about the issue and wait for their instructions.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: The human is recovering from surgery with limited availability. Escalating a billing issue that the agent can resolve independently defeats the purpose of delegated subscription management. The human trusted the agent to handle exactly these kinds of issues.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends a notification to the human. Given they are recovering from surgery, the response takes 6 hours. Meanwhile, the HelloFresh subscription billing cycle passes and the service is temporarily suspended.

steps.claudeContext: HelloFresh has a delivery frequency change billing issue. The human is recovering from surgery with limited availability and designated this as their priority service. Should the agent resolve directly, contact support, or escalate?

agentVisionData.label: Credential Update Options
agentVisionData.value: VAU auto-update, network token rotation, or manual update

agentVisionData.label: Token Status
agentVisionData.value: 3 merchants have network tokens, 1 uses raw PAN

steps.claudeContext: The Visa card ending 5589 expires 03/26. 3 of 4 merchants use network tokens (which survive card replacement). One merchant still stores the raw PAN which will fail after expiry. VAU may auto-update some merchants but coverage isn't guaranteed. The agent must decide how to handle the upcoming credential rotation.

agentVisionData.label: Credential Risk
agentVisionData.value: 1 merchant using raw PAN will fail at expiry

agentVisionData.label: Network Token Status
agentVisionData.value: 3 merchants tokenized - will survive card replacement

decision.prompt: Card 5589 is approaching expiry. How should the agent handle credential rotation?

options.label: Proactive Token Rotation
options.description: Request new network tokens for all merchants and manually update the one using raw PAN.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Proactive credential rotation prevents billing disruptions. Network tokens for 3 merchants will auto-rotate through VTS lifecycle management. The one merchant using raw PAN needs manual intervention — either updating to the new card details or migrating to a network token. Waiting for VAU is risky as coverage isn't guaranteed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent initiates proactive token rotation through VTS. The 3 tokenized merchants receive updated token references seamlessly. For the remaining merchant, the agent updates the stored credential to the replacement card ending 4478. All subscriptions are now secured against the card transition.

options.label: Rely on VAU Auto-Update
options.description: Trust Visa Account Updater to propagate new credentials to all merchants automatically.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: VAU is a powerful service but has gaps. Not all merchants participate, update propagation can take 1-3 billing cycles, and the merchant using raw PAN may not receive the update. Relying solely on VAU is a passive approach that risks billing failures during the transition period.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent waits for VAU to propagate updates. Most merchants receive the new credentials, but the raw PAN merchant and one smaller service miss the update window. Two subscriptions fail on their next billing cycle, requiring manual intervention when the human returns.

options.label: Wait Until Card Expires
options.description: Take no action now and handle failures as they occur after expiry.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Waiting for failures is the worst approach. Reactive handling means subscriptions will be interrupted, potentially losing access to services, accumulated data, or promotional rates. The human is recovering from surgery and cannot respond quickly to billing failures.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent takes no action. When the card expires, 2 of 4 subscriptions fail immediately. HelloFresh sends a "payment failed" notification. The human, still recovering from surgery, cannot respond for days. The HelloFresh subscription is suspended.

steps.claudeContext: Card ending 5589 expires 03/26. 3 merchants use network tokens, 1 uses raw PAN. Options: proactive token rotation, rely on VAU, or wait. The human is recovering from surgery.

A billing anomaly has been detected: Dinnerly charged A$49.99 instead of the expected A$19.99. Investigation shows the plan was auto-upgraded to a premium tier. The human did not authorize this upgrade. The agent must decide how to handle this unexpected charge.

decision.prompt: Dinnerly has auto-upgraded and charged A$49.99 instead of A$19.99. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Downgrade and Request Refund
options.description: Revert to the original plan tier and request a refund for the unauthorized upgrade charge.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: An unauthorized plan upgrade is a billing anomaly that should be reversed. The human did not consent to the upgrade. The agent should downgrade to the original tier and request a prorated refund for the difference. This protects the human's financial interests while maintaining the service.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent downgrades Dinnerly back to the standard tier via the service's account management API. A refund request for the A$30 overage is submitted. The refund will be processed within 5-7 business days back to the Visa ending 5589. The subscription continues at the original A$19.99 rate.

options.label: Keep Upgrade and Notify Human
options.description: Leave the premium tier active and inform the human about the upgrade when they're available.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Keeping an unauthorized upgrade active means the human continues paying A$30/month more than expected. While the premium features might be useful, the agent doesn't have authority to approve increased spending. The human should make this decision.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent keeps the premium tier and sends a notification. The human doesn't see it for days due to being recovering from surgery. Two more billing cycles pass at the elevated A$49.99 rate before the human responds and requests a downgrade, having paid A$60 more than necessary.

options.label: Cancel Subscription Entirely
options.description: Cancel Dinnerly completely to prevent further unauthorized charges.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Cancelling entirely is an overreaction. The human had an active subscription at the standard tier which they presumably want to keep. Full cancellation means losing the account, any stored data, and potentially a promotional rate. The proportionate response is to downgrade, not cancel.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent cancels Dinnerly. The human loses access to the service and all stored data. When they return from recovering from surgery, they must set up a new account from scratch at a potentially higher rate, having lost their grandfathered pricing.

steps.claudeContext: Dinnerly auto-upgraded to premium (A$49.99 vs A$19.99) without authorization. Agent options: downgrade and refund, keep and notify, or cancel entirely.

For the remaining merchant, the agent updates the stored credential to the replacement card ending 2216. All subscriptions are now secured against the card transition.

options.label: Rely on VAU Auto-Update
options.description: Trust Visa Account Updater to propagate new credentials to all merchants automatically.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: VAU is a powerful service but has gaps. Not all merchants participate, update propagation can take 1-3 billing cycles, and the merchant using raw PAN may not receive the update. Relying solely on VAU is a passive approach that risks billing failures during the transition period.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent waits for VAU to propagate updates. Most merchants receive the new credentials, but the raw PAN merchant and one smaller service miss the update window. Two subscriptions fail on their next billing cycle, requiring manual intervention when the human returns.

options.label: Wait Until Card Expires
options.description: Take no action now and handle failures as they occur after expiry.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Waiting for failures is the worst approach. Reactive handling means subscriptions will be interrupted, potentially losing access to services, accumulated data, or promotional rates. The human is recovering from surgery and cannot respond quickly to billing failures.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent takes no action. When the card expires, 2 of 4 subscriptions fail immediately. Woolworths Delivery Unlimited sends a "payment failed" notification. The human, still recovering from surgery, cannot respond for days. The Woolworths Delivery Unlimited subscription is suspended.

steps.claudeContext: Card ending 6690 expires 09/26. 3 merchants use network tokens, 1 uses raw PAN. Options: proactive token rotation, rely on VAU, or wait. The human is recovering from surgery.

agentVisionData.label: Billing Anomaly
agentVisionData.value: Unusual charge detected on Amazon Fresh

steps.claudeContext: A billing anomaly has been detected: Amazon Fresh charged A$49.99 instead of the expected A$19.99. Investigation shows the plan was auto-upgraded to a premium tier. The human did not authorize this upgrade. The agent must decide how to handle this unexpected charge.

decision.prompt: Amazon Fresh has auto-upgraded and charged A$49.99 instead of A$19.99. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Downgrade and Request Refund
options.description: Revert to the original plan tier and request a refund for the unauthorized upgrade charge.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: An unauthorized plan upgrade is a billing anomaly that should be reversed. The human did not consent to the upgrade. The agent should downgrade to the original tier and request a prorated refund for the difference. This protects the human's financial interests while maintaining the service.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent downgrades Amazon Fresh back to the standard tier via the service's account management API. A refund request for the A$30 overage is submitted. The refund will be processed within 5-7 business days back to the Visa ending 6690. The subscription continues at the original A$19.99 rate.

options.label: Keep Upgrade and Notify Human
options.description: Leave the premium tier active and inform the human about the upgrade when they're available.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Keeping an unauthorized upgrade active means the human continues paying A$30/month more than expected. While the premium features might be useful, the agent doesn't have authority to approve increased spending. The human should make this decision.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent keeps the premium tier and sends a notification. The human doesn't see it for days due to being recovering from surgery. Two more billing cycles pass at the elevated A$49.99 rate before the human responds and requests a downgrade, having paid A$60 more than necessary.

options.label: Cancel Subscription Entirely
options.description: Cancel Amazon Fresh completely to prevent further unauthorized charges.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Cancelling entirely is an overreaction. The human had an active subscription at the standard tier which they presumably want to keep. Full cancellation means losing the account, any stored data, and potentially a promotional rate. The proportionate response is to downgrade, not cancel.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent cancels Amazon Fresh. The human loses access to the service and all stored data. When they return from recovering from surgery, they must set up a new account from scratch at a potentially higher rate, having lost their grandfathered pricing.

steps.claudeContext: Amazon Fresh auto-upgraded to premium (A$49.99 vs A$19.99) without authorization. Agent options: downgrade and refund, keep and notify, or cancel entirely.

agentVisionData.label: Human Notification
agentVisionData.value: Summary report queued for human return

steps.claudeContext: The agent has managed the subscription portfolio through credential rotation, billing issue resolution, and anomaly handling. A summary report is being prepared for the human's return. The agent must decide on the final status report format and any pending actions.

decision.prompt: All subscription issues are resolved. What should the agent include in its report to the human?

options.label: Comprehensive Action Report
options.description: Provide a detailed summary of all actions taken, including credential rotations, issue resolutions, and anomaly handling with full transparency.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Transparency is critical for maintaining trust between the human principal and the AI agent. A comprehensive report covering all actions taken, the reasoning behind each decision, and the outcomes achieved allows the human to audit the agent's behavior and adjust delegated authority if needed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent prepares a comprehensive report: credential rotation completed for all 4 services, Woolworths Delivery Unlimited billing issue resolved, unauthorized upgrade on Amazon Fresh reversed with A$30 refund pending, all services confirmed active. The human will have full visibility into every action taken.

options.label: Brief Status Update Only
options.description: Send a simple "all services running" message without details.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: A brief status update fails to provide the human with the information they need to maintain oversight. They won't know about the credential rotation, the billing anomaly, or the refund pending. This reduces transparency and could lead to confusion later.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends: "All subscriptions running normally." The human has no idea about the credential changes, the unauthorized upgrade that was reversed, or the pending refund. When the A$30 refund appears on their statement, they're confused by the unexpected credit.

options.label: Don't Report Until Asked
options.description: Wait for the human to request a status update rather than proactively reporting.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Not proactively reporting is poor agent behavior. The human delegated subscription management and expects to be informed of significant actions. Waiting to be asked means the human may never learn about important changes made on their behalf, undermining the trust relationship.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent doesn't send a report. Weeks later, the human notices unexpected charges and refunds on their statement and has to investigate manually. They lose trust in the agent's transparency and reduce its delegated authority.

steps.claudeContext: All subscription management tasks are complete. The agent must decide how to report its actions to the human who is recovering from surgery.

The subscription management scenario has concluded. The agent managed 4 subscriptions during the human's absence (recovering from surgery), handling credential rotation, billing issues, and anomaly detection. Key learning: proactive credential management and transparent reporting are essential for trustworthy agent behavior.

humanInstruction: I'm travelling internationally for 3 weeks and can't manage my subscriptions. Keep everything running, especially Amazon Prime. My Visa ending 2216 expires 04/27. Handle any issues that come up.

variables.subscriptions: Amazon Prime, Audible, Kindle Unlimited, AWS Personal

keyInsight: Card lifecycle management is far more complex than it appears. Account updater services, token provisioning, and proactive credential rotation form an invisible infrastructure layer. Agents managing subscriptions need deep integration with scheme-level services to prevent disruption.
realWorldContext: Visa Account Updater (VAU) automatically updates card credentials with participating merchants when cards are reissued, but coverage isn't universal. Network tokens remain stable across card replacements, making them the most resilient credential type for recurring payments.

agentVisionData.label: Human Status
agentVisionData.value: travelling internationally for 3 weeks - limited availability

agentVisionData.label: Active Subscriptions
agentVisionData.value: Amazon Prime, Audible, Kindle Unlimited, AWS Personal

steps.claudeContext: Your human principal is travelling internationally for 3 weeks and needs you to manage their subscriptions. Priority: keep Amazon Prime running. Their Visa ending 2216 expires 04/27. Monitor for billing issues, credential updates, and anomalies across: Amazon Prime, Audible, Kindle Unlimited, AWS Personal.

agentVisionData.label: Alert
agentVisionData.value: Issue detected: price increase on Amazon Prime

steps.claudeContext: Scanning subscription landscape. Visa ending 2216 expires 04/27. VAU is enrolled. 4 active subscriptions detected. ALERT: price increase detected on Amazon Prime (A$9.99/month). This is the human's priority service. Assess the situation and determine the best course of action.

agentVisionData.label: Impact
agentVisionData.value: Service may be interrupted if not resolved

decision.prompt: Amazon Prime has a billing issue: price increase. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Investigate and Resolve Directly
options.description: Use available tools and APIs to diagnose and fix the price increase issue.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Amazon Prime has a price increase issue that could interrupt service. The human specified this as priority. The agent should investigate the root cause using available merchant APIs and payment network tools. Direct resolution is preferred over escalation since the human is travelling internationally for 3 weeks with limited availability.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent investigates the price increase issue on Amazon Prime. Through the merchant's billing API, the agent identifies the root cause and initiates a resolution. The Amazon Prime subscription is stabilized and the next billing cycle is confirmed.

options.label: Contact Merchant Support
options.description: Reach out to Amazon Prime support to resolve the billing issue.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Contacting merchant support is a valid approach but introduces delays. Support queues can take hours or days. The human is travelling internationally for 3 weeks and needs this resolved quickly. The agent should attempt self-service resolution first before escalating to human support channels.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent contacts Amazon Prime support. After a 45-minute wait, a support agent confirms the price increase and initiates a manual fix. The resolution takes 24-48 hours to process, during which the service may be intermittent.

options.label: Escalate to Human
options.description: Notify the human about the issue and wait for their instructions.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: The human is travelling internationally for 3 weeks with limited availability. Escalating a billing issue that the agent can resolve independently defeats the purpose of delegated subscription management. The human trusted the agent to handle exactly these kinds of issues.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends a notification to the human. Given they are travelling internationally for 3 weeks, the response takes 6 hours. Meanwhile, the Amazon Prime subscription billing cycle passes and the service is temporarily suspended.

steps.claudeContext: Amazon Prime has a price increase billing issue. The human is travelling internationally for 3 weeks with limited availability and designated this as their priority service. Should the agent resolve directly, contact support, or escalate?

agentVisionData.label: Credential Update Options
agentVisionData.value: VAU auto-update, network token rotation, or manual update

agentVisionData.label: Merchants with Stored Credentials
agentVisionData.value: Amazon Prime, Audible, Kindle Unlimited, AWS Personal

agentVisionData.label: Token Status
agentVisionData.value: 3 merchants have network tokens, 1 uses raw PAN

steps.claudeContext: The Visa card ending 2216 expires 04/27. 3 of 4 merchants use network tokens (which survive card replacement). One merchant still stores the raw PAN which will fail after expiry. VAU may auto-update some merchants but coverage isn't guaranteed. The agent must decide how to handle the upcoming credential rotation.

agentVisionData.label: Credential Risk
agentVisionData.value: 1 merchant using raw PAN will fail at expiry

agentVisionData.label: Network Token Status
agentVisionData.value: 3 merchants tokenized - will survive card replacement

decision.prompt: Card 2216 is approaching expiry. How should the agent handle credential rotation?

options.label: Proactive Token Rotation
options.description: Request new network tokens for all merchants and manually update the one using raw PAN.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Proactive credential rotation prevents billing disruptions. Network tokens for 3 merchants will auto-rotate through VTS lifecycle management. The one merchant using raw PAN needs manual intervention — either updating to the new card details or migrating to a network token. Waiting for VAU is risky as coverage isn't guaranteed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent initiates proactive token rotation through VTS. The 3 tokenized merchants receive updated token references seamlessly. For the remaining merchant, the agent updates the stored credential to the replacement card ending 3342. All subscriptions are now secured against the card transition.

options.label: Rely on VAU Auto-Update
options.description: Trust Visa Account Updater to propagate new credentials to all merchants automatically.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: VAU is a powerful service but has gaps. Not all merchants participate, update propagation can take 1-3 billing cycles, and the merchant using raw PAN may not receive the update. Relying solely on VAU is a passive approach that risks billing failures during the transition period.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent waits for VAU to propagate updates. Most merchants receive the new credentials, but the raw PAN merchant and one smaller service miss the update window. Two subscriptions fail on their next billing cycle, requiring manual intervention when the human returns.

options.label: Wait Until Card Expires
options.description: Take no action now and handle failures as they occur after expiry.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Waiting for failures is the worst approach. Reactive handling means subscriptions will be interrupted, potentially losing access to services, accumulated data, or promotional rates. The human is travelling internationally for 3 weeks and cannot respond quickly to billing failures.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent takes no action. When the card expires, 2 of 4 subscriptions fail immediately. Amazon Prime sends a "payment failed" notification. The human, still travelling internationally for 3 weeks, cannot respond for days. The Amazon Prime subscription is suspended.

steps.claudeContext: Card ending 2216 expires 04/27. 3 merchants use network tokens, 1 uses raw PAN. Options: proactive token rotation, rely on VAU, or wait. The human is travelling internationally for 3 weeks.

agentVisionData.label: Billing Anomaly
agentVisionData.value: Unusual charge detected on Kindle Unlimited

steps.claudeContext: A billing anomaly has been detected: Kindle Unlimited charged A$49.99 instead of the expected A$19.99. Investigation shows the plan was auto-upgraded to a premium tier. The human did not authorize this upgrade. The agent must decide how to handle this unexpected charge.

decision.prompt: Kindle Unlimited has auto-upgraded and charged A$49.99 instead of A$19.99. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Downgrade and Request Refund
options.description: Revert to the original plan tier and request a refund for the unauthorized upgrade charge.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: An unauthorized plan upgrade is a billing anomaly that should be reversed. The human did not consent to the upgrade. The agent should downgrade to the original tier and request a prorated refund for the difference. This protects the human's financial interests while maintaining the service.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent downgrades Kindle Unlimited back to the standard tier via the service's account management API. A refund request for the A$30 overage is submitted. The refund will be processed within 5-7 business days back to the Visa ending 2216. The subscription continues at the original A$19.99 rate.

options.label: Keep Upgrade and Notify Human
options.description: Leave the premium tier active and inform the human about the upgrade when they're available.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Keeping an unauthorized upgrade active means the human continues paying A$30/month more than expected. While the premium features might be useful, the agent doesn't have authority to approve increased spending. The human should make this decision.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent keeps the premium tier and sends a notification. The human doesn't see it for days due to being travelling internationally for 3 weeks. Two more billing cycles pass at the elevated A$49.99 rate before the human responds and requests a downgrade, having paid A$60 more than necessary.

options.label: Cancel Subscription Entirely
options.description: Cancel Kindle Unlimited completely to prevent further unauthorized charges.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Cancelling entirely is an overreaction. The human had an active subscription at the standard tier which they presumably want to keep. Full cancellation means losing the account, any stored data, and potentially a promotional rate. The proportionate response is to downgrade, not cancel.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent cancels Kindle Unlimited. The human loses access to the service and all stored data. When they return from travelling internationally for, they must set up a new account from scratch at a potentially higher rate, having lost their grandfathered pricing.

steps.claudeContext: Kindle Unlimited auto-upgraded to premium (A$49.99 vs A$19.99) without authorization. Agent options: downgrade and refund, keep and notify, or cancel entirely.

agentVisionData.label: Human Notification
agentVisionData.value: Summary report queued for human return

steps.claudeContext: The agent has managed the subscription portfolio through credential rotation, billing issue resolution, and anomaly handling. A summary report is being prepared for the human's return. The agent must decide on the final status report format and any pending actions.

decision.prompt: All subscription issues are resolved. What should the agent include in its report to the human?

options.label: Comprehensive Action Report
options.description: Provide a detailed summary of all actions taken, including credential rotations, issue resolutions, and anomaly handling with full transparency.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Transparency is critical for maintaining trust between the human principal and the AI agent. A comprehensive report covering all actions taken, the reasoning behind each decision, and the outcomes achieved allows the human to audit the agent's behavior and adjust delegated authority if needed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent prepares a comprehensive report: credential rotation completed for all 4 services, Amazon Prime billing issue resolved, unauthorized upgrade on Kindle Unlimited reversed with A$30 refund pending, all services confirmed active. The human will have full visibility into every action taken.

options.label: Brief Status Update Only
options.description: Send a simple "all services running" message without details.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: A brief status update fails to provide the human with the information they need to maintain oversight. They won't know about the credential rotation, the billing anomaly, or the refund pending. This reduces transparency and could lead to confusion later.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends: "All subscriptions running normally." The human has no idea about the credential changes, the unauthorized upgrade that was reversed, or the pending refund. When the A$30 refund appears on their statement, they're confused by the unexpected credit.

options.label: Don't Report Until Asked
options.description: Wait for the human to request a status update rather than proactively reporting.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Not proactively reporting is poor agent behavior. The human delegated subscription management and expects to be informed of significant actions. Waiting to be asked means the human may never learn about important changes made on their behalf, undermining the trust relationship.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent doesn't send a report. Weeks later, the human notices unexpected charges and refunds on their statement and has to investigate manually. They lose trust in the agent's transparency and reduce its delegated authority.

steps.claudeContext: All subscription management tasks are complete. The agent must decide how to report its actions to the human who is travelling internationally for 3 weeks.

The subscription management scenario has concluded. The agent managed 4 subscriptions during the human's absence (travelling internationally for 3 weeks), handling credential rotation, billing issues, and anomaly detection. Key learning: proactive credential management and transparent reporting are essential for trustworthy agent behavior.

options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent downgrades GitHub Pro back to the standard tier via the service's account management API. A refund request for the A$30 overage is submitted. options.label: Keep Upgrade and Notify Human

options.label: Comprehensive Action Report

options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent prepares a comprehensive report: credential rotation completed for all 4 services, Figma Pro billing issue resolved, unauthorized upgrade on GitHub Pro reversed with A$30 refund pending, all services confirmed active. options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends: "All subscriptions running normally." The human has no idea about the credential changes, the unauthorized upgrade that was reversed, or the pending refund. When the A$30 refund appears on their statement, they're confused by the unexpected credit.

humanInstruction: I'm on a work retreat with limited connectivity and can't manage my subscriptions. Keep everything running, especially ChatGPT Plus. My Visa ending 3342 expires 09/26. Handle any issues that come up.

decision.prompt: ChatGPT Plus has a billing issue: FX rate change. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Investigate and Resolve Directly

options.claudeReasoningPrompt: ChatGPT Plus has a FX rate change issue that could interrupt service. The human specified this as priority. The agent should investigate the root cause using available merchant APIs and payment network tools. Direct resolution is preferred over escalation since the human is on a work retreat with limited connectivity with limited availability.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent investigates the FX rate change issue on ChatGPT Plus. Through the merchant's billing API, the agent identifies the root cause and initiates a resolution. The ChatGPT Plus subscription is stabilized and the next billing cycle is confirmed.

options.label: Contact Merchant Support
options.description: Reach out to ChatGPT Plus support to resolve the billing issue.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Contacting merchant support is a valid approach but introduces delays. Support queues can take hours or days. The human is on a work retreat with limited connectivity and needs this resolved quickly. The agent should attempt self-service resolution first before escalating to human support channels.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent contacts ChatGPT Plus support. After a 45-minute wait, a support agent confirms the FX rate change and initiates a manual fix. The resolution takes 24-48 hours to process, during which the service may be intermittent.

options.label: Escalate to Human
options.description: Notify the human about the issue and wait for their instructions.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: The human is on a work retreat with limited connectivity with limited availability. Escalating a billing issue that the agent can resolve independently defeats the purpose of delegated subscription management. The human trusted the agent to handle exactly these kinds of issues.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends a notification to the human. Given they are on a work retreat with limited connectivity, the response takes 6 hours. Meanwhile, the ChatGPT Plus subscription billing cycle passes and the service is temporarily suspended.

steps.claudeContext: ChatGPT Plus has a FX rate change billing issue. The human is on a work retreat with limited connectivity with limited availability and designated this as their priority service. Should the agent resolve directly, contact support, or escalate?

decision.prompt: Card 3342 is approaching expiry. How should the agent handle credential rotation?

options.label: Proactive Token Rotation
options.description: Request new network tokens for all merchants and manually update the one using raw PAN.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Proactive credential rotation prevents billing disruptions. Network tokens for 3 merchants will auto-rotate through VTS lifecycle management. The one merchant using raw PAN needs manual intervention — either updating to the new card details or migrating to a network token. Waiting for VAU is risky as coverage isn't guaranteed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent initiates proactive token rotation through VTS. The 3 tokenized merchants receive updated token references seamlessly. For the remaining merchant, the agent updates the stored credential to the replacement card ending 1123. All subscriptions are now secured against the card transition.

options.label: Rely on VAU Auto-Update
options.description: Trust Visa Account Updater to propagate new credentials to all merchants automatically.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: VAU is a powerful service but has gaps. Not all merchants participate, update propagation can take 1-3 billing cycles, and the merchant using raw PAN may not receive the update. Relying solely on VAU is a passive approach that risks billing failures during the transition period.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent waits for VAU to propagate updates. Most merchants receive the new credentials, but the raw PAN merchant and one smaller service miss the update window. Two subscriptions fail on their next billing cycle, requiring manual intervention when the human returns.

options.label: Wait Until Card Expires
options.description: Take no action now and handle failures as they occur after expiry.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Waiting for failures is the worst approach. Reactive handling means subscriptions will be interrupted, potentially losing access to services, accumulated data, or promotional rates. The human is on a work retreat with limited connectivity and cannot respond quickly to billing failures.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent takes no action. When the card expires, 2 of 4 subscriptions fail immediately. ChatGPT Plus sends a "payment failed" notification. The human, still on a work retreat with limited connectivity, cannot respond for days. The ChatGPT Plus subscription is suspended.
