options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Contacting merchant support is a valid approach but introduces delays. Support queues can take hours or days. The human is at a remote research station and needs this resolved quickly. The agent should attempt self-service resolution first before escalating to human support channels.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent contacts Dropbox Plus support. After a 45-minute wait, a support agent confirms the storage overage charge and initiates a manual fix. The resolution takes 24-48 hours to process, during which the service may be intermittent.

options.label: Escalate to Human
options.description: Notify the human about the issue and wait for their instructions.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: The human is at a remote research station with limited availability. Escalating a billing issue that the agent can resolve independently defeats the purpose of delegated subscription management. The human trusted the agent to handle exactly these kinds of issues.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends a notification to the human. Given they are at a remote research station, the response takes 6 hours. Meanwhile, the Dropbox Plus subscription billing cycle passes and the service is temporarily suspended.

steps.claudeContext: Dropbox Plus has a storage overage charge billing issue. The human is at a remote research station with limited availability and designated this as their priority service. Should the agent resolve directly, contact support, or escalate?

agentVisionData.label: Credential Update Options
agentVisionData.value: VAU auto-update, network token rotation, or manual update

agentVisionData.label: Merchants with Stored Credentials
agentVisionData.value: Dropbox Plus, iCloud+, Google One, OneDrive

agentVisionData.label: Token Status
agentVisionData.value: 3 merchants have network tokens, 1 uses raw PAN

steps.claudeContext: The Visa card ending 5589 expires 01/27. 3 of 4 merchants use network tokens (which survive card replacement). One merchant still stores the raw PAN which will fail after expiry. VAU may auto-update some merchants but coverage isn't guaranteed. The agent must decide how to handle the upcoming credential rotation.

agentVisionData.label: Credential Risk
agentVisionData.value: 1 merchant using raw PAN will fail at expiry

agentVisionData.label: Network Token Status
agentVisionData.value: 3 merchants tokenized - will survive card replacement

decision.prompt: Card 5589 is approaching expiry. How should the agent handle credential rotation?

options.label: Proactive Token Rotation
options.description: Request new network tokens for all merchants and manually update the one using raw PAN.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Proactive credential rotation prevents billing disruptions. Network tokens for 3 merchants will auto-rotate through VTS lifecycle management. The one merchant using raw PAN needs manual intervention — either updating to the new card details or migrating to a network token. Waiting for VAU is risky as coverage isn't guaranteed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent initiates proactive token rotation through VTS. The 3 tokenized merchants receive updated token references seamlessly. For the remaining merchant, the agent updates the stored credential to the replacement card ending 4478. All subscriptions are now secured against the card transition.

options.label: Rely on VAU Auto-Update
options.description: Trust Visa Account Updater to propagate new credentials to all merchants automatically.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: VAU is a powerful service but has gaps. Not all merchants participate, update propagation can take 1-3 billing cycles, and the merchant using raw PAN may not receive the update. Relying solely on VAU is a passive approach that risks billing failures during the transition period.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent waits for VAU to propagate updates. Most merchants receive the new credentials, but the raw PAN merchant and one smaller service miss the update window. Two subscriptions fail on their next billing cycle, requiring manual intervention when the human returns.

options.label: Wait Until Card Expires
options.description: Take no action now and handle failures as they occur after expiry.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Waiting for failures is the worst approach. Reactive handling means subscriptions will be interrupted, potentially losing access to services, accumulated data, or promotional rates. The human is at a remote research station and cannot respond quickly to billing failures.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent takes no action. When the card expires, 2 of 4 subscriptions fail immediately. Dropbox Plus sends a "payment failed" notification. The human, still at a remote research station, cannot respond for days. The Dropbox Plus subscription is suspended.

steps.claudeContext: Card ending 5589 expires 01/27. 3 merchants use network tokens, 1 uses raw PAN. Options: proactive token rotation, rely on VAU, or wait. The human is at a remote research station.

agentVisionData.label: Billing Anomaly
agentVisionData.value: Unusual charge detected on Google One

steps.claudeContext: A billing anomaly has been detected: Google One charged A$49.99 instead of the expected A$19.99. Investigation shows the plan was auto-upgraded to a premium tier. The human did not authorize this upgrade. The agent must decide how to handle this unexpected charge.

decision.prompt: Google One has auto-upgraded and charged A$49.99 instead of A$19.99. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Downgrade and Request Refund
options.description: Revert to the original plan tier and request a refund for the unauthorized upgrade charge.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: An unauthorized plan upgrade is a billing anomaly that should be reversed. The human did not consent to the upgrade. The agent should downgrade to the original tier and request a prorated refund for the difference. This protects the human's financial interests while maintaining the service.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent downgrades Google One back to the standard tier via the service's account management API. A refund request for the A$30 overage is submitted. The refund will be processed within 5-7 business days back to the Visa ending 5589. The subscription continues at the original A$19.99 rate.

options.label: Keep Upgrade and Notify Human
options.description: Leave the premium tier active and inform the human about the upgrade when they're available.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Keeping an unauthorized upgrade active means the human continues paying A$30/month more than expected. While the premium features might be useful, the agent doesn't have authority to approve increased spending. The human should make this decision.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent keeps the premium tier and sends a notification. The human doesn't see it for days due to being at a remote research station. Two more billing cycles pass at the elevated A$49.99 rate before the human responds and requests a downgrade, having paid A$60 more than necessary.

options.label: Cancel Subscription Entirely
options.description: Cancel Google One completely to prevent further unauthorized charges.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Cancelling entirely is an overreaction. The human had an active subscription at the standard tier which they presumably want to keep. Full cancellation means losing the account, any stored data, and potentially a promotional rate. The proportionate response is to downgrade, not cancel.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent cancels Google One. The human loses access to the service and all stored data. When they return from at a remote, they must set up a new account from scratch at a potentially higher rate, having lost their grandfathered pricing.

steps.claudeContext: Google One auto-upgraded to premium (A$49.99 vs A$19.99) without authorization. Agent options: downgrade and refund, keep and notify, or cancel entirely.

agentVisionData.label: Human Notification
agentVisionData.value: Summary report queued for human return

steps.claudeContext: The agent has managed the subscription portfolio through credential rotation, billing issue resolution, and anomaly handling. A summary report is being prepared for the human's return. The agent must decide on the final status report format and any pending actions.

decision.prompt: All subscription issues are resolved. What should the agent include in its report to the human?

options.label: Comprehensive Action Report
options.description: Provide a detailed summary of all actions taken, including credential rotations, issue resolutions, and anomaly handling with full transparency.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Transparency is critical for maintaining trust between the human principal and the AI agent. A comprehensive report covering all actions taken, the reasoning behind each decision, and the outcomes achieved allows the human to audit the agent's behavior and adjust delegated authority if needed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent prepares a comprehensive report: credential rotation completed for all 4 services, Dropbox Plus billing issue resolved, unauthorized upgrade on Google One reversed with A$30 refund pending, all services confirmed active. The human will have full visibility into every action taken.

options.label: Brief Status Update Only
options.description: Send a simple "all services running" message without details.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: A brief status update fails to provide the human with the information they need to maintain oversight. They won't know about the credential rotation, the billing anomaly, or the refund pending. This reduces transparency and could lead to confusion later.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends: "All subscriptions running normally." The human has no idea about the credential changes, the unauthorized upgrade that was reversed, or the pending refund. When the A$30 refund appears on their statement, they're confused by the unexpected credit.

options.label: Don't Report Until Asked
options.description: Wait for the human to request a status update rather than proactively reporting.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Not proactively reporting is poor agent behavior. The human delegated subscription management and expects to be informed of significant actions. Waiting to be asked means the human may never learn about important changes made on their behalf, undermining the trust relationship.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent doesn't send a report. Weeks later, the human notices unexpected charges and refunds on their statement and has to investigate manually. They lose trust in the agent's transparency and reduce its delegated authority.

steps.claudeContext: All subscription management tasks are complete. The agent must decide how to report its actions to the human who is at a remote research station.

The subscription management scenario has concluded. The agent managed 4 subscriptions during the human's absence (at a remote research station), handling credential rotation, billing issues, and anomaly detection. Key learning: proactive credential management and transparent reporting are essential for trustworthy agent behavior.

humanInstruction: I'm recovering from surgery and can't manage my subscriptions. Keep everything running, especially NordVPN. My Visa ending 1123 expires 04/27. Handle any issues that come up.

variables.subscriptions: NordVPN, 1Password, Bitwarden, ExpressVPN

keyInsight: Card lifecycle management is far more complex than it appears. Account updater services, token provisioning, and proactive credential rotation form an invisible infrastructure layer. Agents managing subscriptions need deep integration with scheme-level services to prevent disruption.

steps.claudeContext: The Visa card ending 2216 expires 12/26. 3 of 4 merchants use network tokens (which survive card replacement). One merchant still stores the raw PAN which will fail after expiry. VAU may auto-update some merchants but coverage isn't guaranteed. The agent must decide how to handle the upcoming credential rotation.

agentVisionData.label: Credential Risk
agentVisionData.value: 1 merchant using raw PAN will fail at expiry

agentVisionData.label: Network Token Status
agentVisionData.value: 3 merchants tokenized - will survive card replacement

decision.prompt: Card 2216 is approaching expiry. How should the agent handle credential rotation?

options.label: Proactive Token Rotation
options.description: Request new network tokens for all merchants and manually update the one using raw PAN.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Proactive credential rotation prevents billing disruptions. Network tokens for 3 merchants will auto-rotate through VTS lifecycle management. The one merchant using raw PAN needs manual intervention — either updating to the new card details or migrating to a network token. Waiting for VAU is risky as coverage isn't guaranteed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent initiates proactive token rotation through VTS. The 3 tokenized merchants receive updated token references seamlessly. For the remaining merchant, the agent updates the stored credential to the replacement card ending 3342. All subscriptions are now secured against the card transition.

options.label: Rely on VAU Auto-Update
options.description: Trust Visa Account Updater to propagate new credentials to all merchants automatically.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: VAU is a powerful service but has gaps. Not all merchants participate, update propagation can take 1-3 billing cycles, and the merchant using raw PAN may not receive the update. Relying solely on VAU is a passive approach that risks billing failures during the transition period.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent waits for VAU to propagate updates. Most merchants receive the new credentials, but the raw PAN merchant and one smaller service miss the update window. Two subscriptions fail on their next billing cycle, requiring manual intervention when the human returns.

options.label: Wait Until Card Expires
options.description: Take no action now and handle failures as they occur after expiry.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Waiting for failures is the worst approach. Reactive handling means subscriptions will be interrupted, potentially losing access to services, accumulated data, or promotional rates. The human is at a remote research station and cannot respond quickly to billing failures.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent takes no action. When the card expires, 2 of 4 subscriptions fail immediately. HelloFresh sends a "payment failed" notification. The human, still at a remote research station, cannot respond for days. The HelloFresh subscription is suspended.

steps.claudeContext: Card ending 2216 expires 12/26. 3 merchants use network tokens, 1 uses raw PAN. Options: proactive token rotation, rely on VAU, or wait. The human is at a remote research station.

A billing anomaly has been detected: Dinnerly charged A$49.99 instead of the expected A$19.99. Investigation shows the plan was auto-upgraded to a premium tier. The human did not authorize this upgrade. The agent must decide how to handle this unexpected charge.

decision.prompt: Dinnerly has auto-upgraded and charged A$49.99 instead of A$19.99. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Downgrade and Request Refund
options.description: Revert to the original plan tier and request a refund for the unauthorized upgrade charge.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: An unauthorized plan upgrade is a billing anomaly that should be reversed. The human did not consent to the upgrade. The agent should downgrade to the original tier and request a prorated refund for the difference. This protects the human's financial interests while maintaining the service.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent downgrades Dinnerly back to the standard tier via the service's account management API. A refund request for the A$30 overage is submitted. The refund will be processed within 5-7 business days back to the Visa ending 2216. The subscription continues at the original A$19.99 rate.

options.label: Keep Upgrade and Notify Human
options.description: Leave the premium tier active and inform the human about the upgrade when they're available.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Keeping an unauthorized upgrade active means the human continues paying A$30/month more than expected. While the premium features might be useful, the agent doesn't have authority to approve increased spending. The human should make this decision.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent keeps the premium tier and sends a notification. The human doesn't see it for days due to being at a remote research station. Two more billing cycles pass at the elevated A$49.99 rate before the human responds and requests a downgrade, having paid A$60 more than necessary.

options.label: Cancel Subscription Entirely
options.description: Cancel Dinnerly completely to prevent further unauthorized charges.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Cancelling entirely is an overreaction. The human had an active subscription at the standard tier which they presumably want to keep. Full cancellation means losing the account, any stored data, and potentially a promotional rate. The proportionate response is to downgrade, not cancel.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent cancels Dinnerly. The human loses access to the service and all stored data. When they return from at a remote, they must set up a new account from scratch at a potentially higher rate, having lost their grandfathered pricing.

steps.claudeContext: Dinnerly auto-upgraded to premium (A$49.99 vs A$19.99) without authorization. Agent options: downgrade and refund, keep and notify, or cancel entirely.

agentVisionData.label: Human Notification
agentVisionData.value: Summary report queued for human return

steps.claudeContext: The agent has managed the subscription portfolio through credential rotation, billing issue resolution, and anomaly handling. A summary report is being prepared for the human's return. The agent must decide on the final status report format and any pending actions.

decision.prompt: All subscription issues are resolved. What should the agent include in its report to the human?

options.label: Comprehensive Action Report
options.description: Provide a detailed summary of all actions taken, including credential rotations, issue resolutions, and anomaly handling with full transparency.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Transparency is critical for maintaining trust between the human principal and the AI agent. A comprehensive report covering all actions taken, the reasoning behind each decision, and the outcomes achieved allows the human to audit the agent's behavior and adjust delegated authority if needed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent prepares a comprehensive report: credential rotation completed for all 4 services, HelloFresh billing issue resolved, unauthorized upgrade on Dinnerly reversed with A$30 refund pending, all services confirmed active. The human will have full visibility into every action taken.

options.label: Brief Status Update Only
options.description: Send a simple "all services running" message without details.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: A brief status update fails to provide the human with the information they need to maintain oversight. They won't know about the credential rotation, the billing anomaly, or the refund pending. This reduces transparency and could lead to confusion later.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends: "All subscriptions running normally." The human has no idea about the credential changes, the unauthorized upgrade that was reversed, or the pending refund. When the A$30 refund appears on their statement, they're confused by the unexpected credit.

options.label: Don't Report Until Asked
options.description: Wait for the human to request a status update rather than proactively reporting.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Not proactively reporting is poor agent behavior. The human delegated subscription management and expects to be informed of significant actions. Waiting to be asked means the human may never learn about important changes made on their behalf, undermining the trust relationship.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent doesn't send a report. Weeks later, the human notices unexpected charges and refunds on their statement and has to investigate manually. They lose trust in the agent's transparency and reduce its delegated authority.

steps.claudeContext: All subscription management tasks are complete. The agent must decide how to report its actions to the human who is at a remote research station.

The subscription management scenario has concluded. The agent managed 4 subscriptions during the human's absence (at a remote research station), handling credential rotation, billing issues, and anomaly detection. Key learning: proactive credential management and transparent reporting are essential for trustworthy agent behavior.

options.label: Comprehensive Action Report
options.description: Provide a detailed summary of all actions taken, including credential rotations, issue resolutions, and anomaly handling with full transparency.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Transparency is critical for maintaining trust between the human principal and the AI agent. A comprehensive report covering all actions taken, the reasoning behind each decision, and the outcomes achieved allows the human to audit the agent's behavior and adjust delegated authority if needed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent prepares a comprehensive report: credential rotation completed for all 4 services, Woolworths Delivery Unlimited billing issue resolved, unauthorized upgrade on Amazon Fresh reversed with A$30 refund pending, all services confirmed active. The human will have full visibility into every action taken.

options.label: Brief Status Update Only
options.description: Send a simple "all services running" message without details.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: A brief status update fails to provide the human with the information they need to maintain oversight. They won't know about the credential rotation, the billing anomaly, or the refund pending. This reduces transparency and could lead to confusion later.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends: "All subscriptions running normally." The human has no idea about the credential changes, the unauthorized upgrade that was reversed, or the pending refund. When the A$30 refund appears on their statement, they're confused by the unexpected credit.

options.label: Don't Report Until Asked

steps.claudeContext: All subscription management tasks are complete. The agent must decide how to report its actions to the human who is at a remote research station.

humanInstruction: I'm travelling internationally for 3 weeks and can't manage my subscriptions. Keep everything running, especially Netflix Premium. My Visa ending 3342 expires 03/26. Handle any issues that come up.

variables.subscriptions: Netflix Premium, Spotify Family, Adobe Creative Cloud, Microsoft 365

keyInsight: Card lifecycle management is far more complex than it appears. Account updater services, token provisioning, and proactive credential rotation form an invisible infrastructure layer. Agents managing subscriptions need deep integration with scheme-level services to prevent disruption.
realWorldContext: Visa Account Updater (VAU) automatically updates card credentials with participating merchants when cards are reissued, but coverage isn't universal. Network tokens remain stable across card replacements, making them the most resilient credential type for recurring payments.

agentVisionData.label: Human Status
agentVisionData.value: travelling internationally for 3 weeks - limited availability

agentVisionData.label: Active Subscriptions
agentVisionData.value: Netflix Premium, Spotify Family, Adobe Creative Cloud, Microsoft 365

steps.claudeContext: Your human principal is travelling internationally for 3 weeks and needs you to manage their subscriptions. Priority: keep Netflix Premium running. Their Visa ending 3342 expires 03/26. Monitor for billing issues, credential updates, and anomalies across: Netflix Premium, Spotify Family, Adobe Creative Cloud, Microsoft 365.

agentVisionData.label: Alert
agentVisionData.value: Issue detected: card expiry on Netflix Premium

steps.claudeContext: Scanning subscription landscape. Visa ending 3342 expires 03/26. VAU is enrolled. 4 active subscriptions detected. ALERT: card expiry detected on Netflix Premium (A$32.99/month). This is the human's priority service. Assess the situation and determine the best course of action.

agentVisionData.label: Impact
agentVisionData.value: Service may be interrupted if not resolved

decision.prompt: Netflix Premium has a billing issue: card expiry. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Investigate and Resolve Directly
options.description: Use available tools and APIs to diagnose and fix the card expiry issue.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Netflix Premium has a card expiry issue that could interrupt service. The human specified this as priority. The agent should investigate the root cause using available merchant APIs and payment network tools. Direct resolution is preferred over escalation since the human is travelling internationally for 3 weeks with limited availability.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent investigates the card expiry issue on Netflix Premium. Through the merchant's billing API, the agent identifies the root cause and initiates a resolution. The Netflix Premium subscription is stabilized and the next billing cycle is confirmed.

options.label: Contact Merchant Support
options.description: Reach out to Netflix Premium support to resolve the billing issue.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Contacting merchant support is a valid approach but introduces delays. Support queues can take hours or days. The human is travelling internationally for 3 weeks and needs this resolved quickly. The agent should attempt self-service resolution first before escalating to human support channels.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent contacts Netflix Premium support. After a 45-minute wait, a support agent confirms the card expiry and initiates a manual fix. The resolution takes 24-48 hours to process, during which the service may be intermittent.

options.label: Escalate to Human
options.description: Notify the human about the issue and wait for their instructions.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: The human is travelling internationally for 3 weeks with limited availability. Escalating a billing issue that the agent can resolve independently defeats the purpose of delegated subscription management. The human trusted the agent to handle exactly these kinds of issues.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends a notification to the human. Given they are travelling internationally for 3 weeks, the response takes 6 hours. Meanwhile, the Netflix Premium subscription billing cycle passes and the service is temporarily suspended.

steps.claudeContext: Netflix Premium has a card expiry billing issue. The human is travelling internationally for 3 weeks with limited availability and designated this as their priority service. Should the agent resolve directly, contact support, or escalate?

agentVisionData.label: Credential Update Options
agentVisionData.value: VAU auto-update, network token rotation, or manual update

agentVisionData.label: Merchants with Stored Credentials
agentVisionData.value: Netflix Premium, Spotify Family, Adobe Creative Cloud, Microsoft 365

agentVisionData.label: Token Status
agentVisionData.value: 3 merchants have network tokens, 1 uses raw PAN

steps.claudeContext: The Visa card ending 3342 expires 03/26. 3 of 4 merchants use network tokens (which survive card replacement). One merchant still stores the raw PAN which will fail after expiry. VAU may auto-update some merchants but coverage isn't guaranteed. The agent must decide how to handle the upcoming credential rotation.

agentVisionData.label: Credential Risk
agentVisionData.value: 1 merchant using raw PAN will fail at expiry

agentVisionData.label: Network Token Status
agentVisionData.value: 3 merchants tokenized - will survive card replacement

decision.prompt: Card 3342 is approaching expiry. How should the agent handle credential rotation?

options.label: Proactive Token Rotation
options.description: Request new network tokens for all merchants and manually update the one using raw PAN.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Proactive credential rotation prevents billing disruptions. Network tokens for 3 merchants will auto-rotate through VTS lifecycle management. The one merchant using raw PAN needs manual intervention — either updating to the new card details or migrating to a network token. Waiting for VAU is risky as coverage isn't guaranteed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent initiates proactive token rotation through VTS. The 3 tokenized merchants receive updated token references seamlessly. For the remaining merchant, the agent updates the stored credential to the replacement card ending 1123. All subscriptions are now secured against the card transition.

options.label: Rely on VAU Auto-Update
options.description: Trust Visa Account Updater to propagate new credentials to all merchants automatically.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: VAU is a powerful service but has gaps. Not all merchants participate, update propagation can take 1-3 billing cycles, and the merchant using raw PAN may not receive the update. Relying solely on VAU is a passive approach that risks billing failures during the transition period.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent waits for VAU to propagate updates. Most merchants receive the new credentials, but the raw PAN merchant and one smaller service miss the update window. Two subscriptions fail on their next billing cycle, requiring manual intervention when the human returns.

options.label: Wait Until Card Expires
options.description: Take no action now and handle failures as they occur after expiry.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Waiting for failures is the worst approach. Reactive handling means subscriptions will be interrupted, potentially losing access to services, accumulated data, or promotional rates. The human is travelling internationally for 3 weeks and cannot respond quickly to billing failures.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent takes no action. When the card expires, 2 of 4 subscriptions fail immediately. Netflix Premium sends a "payment failed" notification. The human, still travelling internationally for 3 weeks, cannot respond for days. The Netflix Premium subscription is suspended.

steps.claudeContext: Card ending 3342 expires 03/26. 3 merchants use network tokens, 1 uses raw PAN. Options: proactive token rotation, rely on VAU, or wait. The human is travelling internationally for 3 weeks.

agentVisionData.label: Billing Anomaly
agentVisionData.value: Unusual charge detected on Adobe Creative Cloud

steps.claudeContext: A billing anomaly has been detected: Adobe Creative Cloud charged A$49.99 instead of the expected A$19.99. Investigation shows the plan was auto-upgraded to a premium tier. The human did not authorize this upgrade. The agent must decide how to handle this unexpected charge.

agentVisionData.label: Unauthorized Upgrade
agentVisionData.value: Adobe Creative Cloud upgraded without consent

decision.prompt: Adobe Creative Cloud has auto-upgraded and charged A$49.99 instead of A$19.99. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Downgrade and Request Refund
options.description: Revert to the original plan tier and request a refund for the unauthorized upgrade charge.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: An unauthorized plan upgrade is a billing anomaly that should be reversed. The human did not consent to the upgrade. The agent should downgrade to the original tier and request a prorated refund for the difference. This protects the human's financial interests while maintaining the service.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent downgrades Adobe Creative Cloud back to the standard tier via the service's account management API. A refund request for the A$30 overage is submitted. The refund will be processed within 5-7 business days back to the Visa ending 3342. The subscription continues at the original A$19.99 rate.

options.label: Keep Upgrade and Notify Human
options.description: Leave the premium tier active and inform the human about the upgrade when they're available.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Keeping an unauthorized upgrade active means the human continues paying A$30/month more than expected. While the premium features might be useful, the agent doesn't have authority to approve increased spending. The human should make this decision.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent keeps the premium tier and sends a notification. The human doesn't see it for days due to being travelling internationally for 3 weeks. Two more billing cycles pass at the elevated A$49.99 rate before the human responds and requests a downgrade, having paid A$60 more than necessary.

options.label: Cancel Subscription Entirely
options.description: Cancel Adobe Creative Cloud completely to prevent further unauthorized charges.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Cancelling entirely is an overreaction. The human had an active subscription at the standard tier which they presumably want to keep. Full cancellation means losing the account, any stored data, and potentially a promotional rate. The proportionate response is to downgrade, not cancel.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent cancels Adobe Creative Cloud. The human loses access to the service and all stored data. When they return from travelling internationally for, they must set up a new account from scratch at a potentially higher rate, having lost their grandfathered pricing.

steps.claudeContext: Adobe Creative Cloud auto-upgraded to premium (A$49.99 vs A$19.99) without authorization. Agent options: downgrade and refund, keep and notify, or cancel entirely.

options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Proactive credential rotation prevents billing disruptions. Network tokens for 3 merchants will auto-rotate through VTS lifecycle management. The one merchant using raw PAN needs manual intervention — either updating to the new card details or migrating to a network token. Waiting for VAU is risky as coverage isn't guaranteed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent initiates proactive token rotation through VTS. The 3 tokenized merchants receive updated token references seamlessly. For the remaining merchant, the agent updates the stored credential to the replacement card ending 4478. All subscriptions are now secured against the card transition.

options.label: Rely on VAU Auto-Update
options.description: Trust Visa Account Updater to propagate new credentials to all merchants automatically.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: VAU is a powerful service but has gaps. Not all merchants participate, update propagation can take 1-3 billing cycles, and the merchant using raw PAN may not receive the update. Relying solely on VAU is a passive approach that risks billing failures during the transition period.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent waits for VAU to propagate updates. Most merchants receive the new credentials, but the raw PAN merchant and one smaller service miss the update window. Two subscriptions fail on their next billing cycle, requiring manual intervention when the human returns.

options.label: Wait Until Card Expires
options.description: Take no action now and handle failures as they occur after expiry.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Waiting for failures is the worst approach. Reactive handling means subscriptions will be interrupted, potentially losing access to services, accumulated data, or promotional rates. The human is on parental leave and cannot respond quickly to billing failures.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent takes no action. When the card expires, 2 of 4 subscriptions fail immediately. Amazon Prime sends a "payment failed" notification. The human, still on parental leave, cannot respond for days. The Amazon Prime subscription is suspended.

steps.claudeContext: Card ending 5589 expires 09/26. 3 merchants use network tokens, 1 uses raw PAN. Options: proactive token rotation, rely on VAU, or wait. The human is on parental leave.

agentVisionData.label: Billing Anomaly
agentVisionData.value: Unusual charge detected on Kindle Unlimited

steps.claudeContext: A billing anomaly has been detected: Kindle Unlimited charged A$49.99 instead of the expected A$19.99. Investigation shows the plan was auto-upgraded to a premium tier. The human did not authorize this upgrade. The agent must decide how to handle this unexpected charge.

decision.prompt: Kindle Unlimited has auto-upgraded and charged A$49.99 instead of A$19.99. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Downgrade and Request Refund
options.description: Revert to the original plan tier and request a refund for the unauthorized upgrade charge.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: An unauthorized plan upgrade is a billing anomaly that should be reversed. The human did not consent to the upgrade. The agent should downgrade to the original tier and request a prorated refund for the difference. This protects the human's financial interests while maintaining the service.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent downgrades Kindle Unlimited back to the standard tier via the service's account management API. A refund request for the A$30 overage is submitted. The refund will be processed within 5-7 business days back to the Visa ending 5589. The subscription continues at the original A$19.99 rate.

options.label: Keep Upgrade and Notify Human
options.description: Leave the premium tier active and inform the human about the upgrade when they're available.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Keeping an unauthorized upgrade active means the human continues paying A$30/month more than expected. While the premium features might be useful, the agent doesn't have authority to approve increased spending. The human should make this decision.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent keeps the premium tier and sends a notification. The human doesn't see it for days due to being on parental leave. Two more billing cycles pass at the elevated A$49.99 rate before the human responds and requests a downgrade, having paid A$60 more than necessary.

options.label: Cancel Subscription Entirely
options.description: Cancel Kindle Unlimited completely to prevent further unauthorized charges.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Cancelling entirely is an overreaction. The human had an active subscription at the standard tier which they presumably want to keep. Full cancellation means losing the account, any stored data, and potentially a promotional rate. The proportionate response is to downgrade, not cancel.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent cancels Kindle Unlimited. The human loses access to the service and all stored data. When they return from on parental leave, they must set up a new account from scratch at a potentially higher rate, having lost their grandfathered pricing.

steps.claudeContext: Kindle Unlimited auto-upgraded to premium (A$49.99 vs A$19.99) without authorization. Agent options: downgrade and refund, keep and notify, or cancel entirely.

agentVisionData.label: Human Notification
agentVisionData.value: Summary report queued for human return

steps.claudeContext: The agent has managed the subscription portfolio through credential rotation, billing issue resolution, and anomaly handling. A summary report is being prepared for the human's return. The agent must decide on the final status report format and any pending actions.

decision.prompt: All subscription issues are resolved. What should the agent include in its report to the human?

options.label: Comprehensive Action Report
options.description: Provide a detailed summary of all actions taken, including credential rotations, issue resolutions, and anomaly handling with full transparency.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Transparency is critical for maintaining trust between the human principal and the AI agent. A comprehensive report covering all actions taken, the reasoning behind each decision, and the outcomes achieved allows the human to audit the agent's behavior and adjust delegated authority if needed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent prepares a comprehensive report: credential rotation completed for all 4 services, Amazon Prime billing issue resolved, unauthorized upgrade on Kindle Unlimited reversed with A$30 refund pending, all services confirmed active. The human will have full visibility into every action taken.

options.label: Brief Status Update Only
options.description: Send a simple "all services running" message without details.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: A brief status update fails to provide the human with the information they need to maintain oversight. They won't know about the credential rotation, the billing anomaly, or the refund pending. This reduces transparency and could lead to confusion later.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends: "All subscriptions running normally." The human has no idea about the credential changes, the unauthorized upgrade that was reversed, or the pending refund. When the A$30 refund appears on their statement, they're confused by the unexpected credit.

options.label: Don't Report Until Asked
options.description: Wait for the human to request a status update rather than proactively reporting.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Not proactively reporting is poor agent behavior. The human delegated subscription management and expects to be informed of significant actions. Waiting to be asked means the human may never learn about important changes made on their behalf, undermining the trust relationship.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent doesn't send a report. Weeks later, the human notices unexpected charges and refunds on their statement and has to investigate manually. They lose trust in the agent's transparency and reduce its delegated authority.

steps.claudeContext: All subscription management tasks are complete. The agent must decide how to report its actions to the human who is on parental leave.

The subscription management scenario has concluded. The agent managed 4 subscriptions during the human's absence (on parental leave), handling credential rotation, billing issues, and anomaly detection. Key learning: proactive credential management and transparent reporting are essential for trustworthy agent behavior.

humanInstruction: I'm travelling internationally for 3 weeks and can't manage my subscriptions. Keep everything running, especially Stan. My Visa ending 1123 expires 12/26. Handle any issues that come up.

variables.subscriptions: Stan, Binge, Paramount+, Apple TV+

keyInsight: Card lifecycle management is far more complex than it appears. Account updater services, token provisioning, and proactive credential rotation form an invisible infrastructure layer. Agents managing subscriptions need deep integration with scheme-level services to prevent disruption.
realWorldContext: Visa Account Updater (VAU) automatically updates card credentials with participating merchants when cards are reissued, but coverage isn't universal. Network tokens remain stable across card replacements, making them the most resilient credential type for recurring payments.

agentVisionData.label: Human Status
agentVisionData.value: travelling internationally for 3 weeks - limited availability

Monitor for billing issues, credential updates, and anomalies across: Stan, Binge, Paramount+, Apple TV+.

agentVisionData.label: Alert
agentVisionData.value: Issue detected: duplicate charge on Stan

steps.claudeContext: Scanning subscription landscape. Visa ending 1123 expires 12/26. VAU is enrolled. 4 active subscriptions detected. ALERT: duplicate charge detected on Stan (A$24/month). This is the human's priority service. Assess the situation and determine the best course of action.

agentVisionData.label: Impact
agentVisionData.value: Service may be interrupted if not resolved

decision.prompt: Stan has a billing issue: duplicate charge. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Investigate and Resolve Directly
options.description: Use available tools and APIs to diagnose and fix the duplicate charge issue.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Stan has a duplicate charge issue that could interrupt service. The human specified this as priority. The agent should investigate the root cause using available merchant APIs and payment network tools. Direct resolution is preferred over escalation since the human is travelling internationally for 3 weeks with limited availability.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent investigates the duplicate charge issue on Stan. Through the merchant's billing API, the agent identifies the root cause and initiates a resolution. The Stan subscription is stabilized and the next billing cycle is confirmed.

options.label: Contact Merchant Support
options.description: Reach out to Stan support to resolve the billing issue.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Contacting merchant support is a valid approach but introduces delays. Support queues can take hours or days. The human is travelling internationally for 3 weeks and needs this resolved quickly. The agent should attempt self-service resolution first before escalating to human support channels.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent contacts Stan support. After a 45-minute wait, a support agent confirms the duplicate charge and initiates a manual fix. The resolution takes 24-48 hours to process, during which the service may be intermittent.

options.label: Escalate to Human
options.description: Notify the human about the issue and wait for their instructions.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: The human is travelling internationally for 3 weeks with limited availability. Escalating a billing issue that the agent can resolve independently defeats the purpose of delegated subscription management. The human trusted the agent to handle exactly these kinds of issues.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends a notification to the human. Given they are travelling internationally for 3 weeks, the response takes 6 hours. Meanwhile, the Stan subscription billing cycle passes and the service is temporarily suspended.

steps.claudeContext: Stan has a duplicate charge billing issue. The human is travelling internationally for 3 weeks with limited availability and designated this as their priority service. Should the agent resolve directly, contact support, or escalate?

decision.prompt: ChatGPT Plus has a billing issue: FX rate change. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Investigate and Resolve Directly

options.claudeReasoningPrompt: ChatGPT Plus has a FX rate change issue that could interrupt service. The human specified this as priority. The agent should investigate the root cause using available merchant APIs and payment network tools. Direct resolution is preferred over escalation since the human is on parental leave with limited availability.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent investigates the FX rate change issue on ChatGPT Plus. Through the merchant's billing API, the agent identifies the root cause and initiates a resolution. The ChatGPT Plus subscription is stabilized and the next billing cycle is confirmed.

options.label: Contact Merchant Support
options.description: Reach out to ChatGPT Plus support to resolve the billing issue.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Contacting merchant support is a valid approach but introduces delays. Support queues can take hours or days. The human is on parental leave and needs this resolved quickly. The agent should attempt self-service resolution first before escalating to human support channels.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent contacts ChatGPT Plus support. After a 45-minute wait, a support agent confirms the FX rate change and initiates a manual fix. The resolution takes 24-48 hours to process, during which the service may be intermittent.

options.label: Escalate to Human
options.description: Notify the human about the issue and wait for their instructions.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: The human is on parental leave with limited availability. Escalating a billing issue that the agent can resolve independently defeats the purpose of delegated subscription management. The human trusted the agent to handle exactly these kinds of issues.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends a notification to the human. Given they are on parental leave, the response takes 6 hours. Meanwhile, the ChatGPT Plus subscription billing cycle passes and the service is temporarily suspended.

steps.claudeContext: ChatGPT Plus has a FX rate change billing issue. The human is on parental leave with limited availability and designated this as their priority service. Should the agent resolve directly, contact support, or escalate?

decision.prompt: Card 4478 is approaching expiry. How should the agent handle credential rotation?

options.label: Proactive Token Rotation
options.description: Request new network tokens for all merchants and manually update the one using raw PAN.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Proactive credential rotation prevents billing disruptions. Network tokens for 3 merchants will auto-rotate through VTS lifecycle management. The one merchant using raw PAN needs manual intervention — either updating to the new card details or migrating to a network token. Waiting for VAU is risky as coverage isn't guaranteed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent initiates proactive token rotation through VTS. The 3 tokenized merchants receive updated token references seamlessly. For the remaining merchant, the agent updates the stored credential to the replacement card ending 9157. All subscriptions are now secured against the card transition.

options.label: Rely on VAU Auto-Update
options.description: Trust Visa Account Updater to propagate new credentials to all merchants automatically.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: VAU is a powerful service but has gaps. Not all merchants participate, update propagation can take 1-3 billing cycles, and the merchant using raw PAN may not receive the update. Relying solely on VAU is a passive approach that risks billing failures during the transition period.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent waits for VAU to propagate updates. Most merchants receive the new credentials, but the raw PAN merchant and one smaller service miss the update window. Two subscriptions fail on their next billing cycle, requiring manual intervention when the human returns.

options.label: Wait Until Card Expires
options.description: Take no action now and handle failures as they occur after expiry.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Waiting for failures is the worst approach. Reactive handling means subscriptions will be interrupted, potentially losing access to services, accumulated data, or promotional rates. The human is on parental leave and cannot respond quickly to billing failures.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent takes no action. When the card expires, 2 of 4 subscriptions fail immediately. ChatGPT Plus sends a "payment failed" notification. The human, still on parental leave, cannot respond for days. The ChatGPT Plus subscription is suspended.

steps.claudeContext: Card ending 4478 expires 04/27. 3 merchants use network tokens, 1 uses raw PAN. Options: proactive token rotation, rely on VAU, or wait. The human is on parental leave.

A billing anomaly has been detected: Midjourney charged A$49.99 instead of the expected A$19.99. Investigation shows the plan was auto-upgraded to a premium tier. The human did not authorize this upgrade. The agent must decide how to handle this unexpected charge.

decision.prompt: Midjourney has auto-upgraded and charged A$49.99 instead of A$19.99. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Downgrade and Request Refund
options.description: Revert to the original plan tier and request a refund for the unauthorized upgrade charge.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: An unauthorized plan upgrade is a billing anomaly that should be reversed. The human did not consent to the upgrade. The agent should downgrade to the original tier and request a prorated refund for the difference. This protects the human's financial interests while maintaining the service.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent downgrades Midjourney back to the standard tier via the service's account management API. A refund request for the A$30 overage is submitted. The refund will be processed within 5-7 business days back to the Visa ending 4478. The subscription continues at the original A$19.99 rate.

options.label: Keep Upgrade and Notify Human
options.description: Leave the premium tier active and inform the human about the upgrade when they're available.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Keeping an unauthorized upgrade active means the human continues paying A$30/month more than expected. While the premium features might be useful, the agent doesn't have authority to approve increased spending. The human should make this decision.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent keeps the premium tier and sends a notification. The human doesn't see it for days due to being on parental leave. Two more billing cycles pass at the elevated A$49.99 rate before the human responds and requests a downgrade, having paid A$60 more than necessary.

options.label: Cancel Subscription Entirely
options.description: Cancel Midjourney completely to prevent further unauthorized charges.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Cancelling entirely is an overreaction. The human had an active subscription at the standard tier which they presumably want to keep. Full cancellation means losing the account, any stored data, and potentially a promotional rate. The proportionate response is to downgrade, not cancel.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent cancels Midjourney. The human loses access to the service and all stored data. When they return from on parental leave, they must set up a new account from scratch at a potentially higher rate, having lost their grandfathered pricing.

steps.claudeContext: Midjourney auto-upgraded to premium (A$49.99 vs A$19.99) without authorization. Agent options: downgrade and refund, keep and notify, or cancel entirely.

agentVisionData.label: Human Notification
agentVisionData.value: Summary report queued for human return

steps.claudeContext: The agent has managed the subscription portfolio through credential rotation, billing issue resolution, and anomaly handling. A summary report is being prepared for the human's return. The agent must decide on the final status report format and any pending actions.

decision.prompt: All subscription issues are resolved. What should the agent include in its report to the human?

options.label: Comprehensive Action Report
options.description: Provide a detailed summary of all actions taken, including credential rotations, issue resolutions, and anomaly handling with full transparency.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Transparency is critical for maintaining trust between the human principal and the AI agent. A comprehensive report covering all actions taken, the reasoning behind each decision, and the outcomes achieved allows the human to audit the agent's behavior and adjust delegated authority if needed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent prepares a comprehensive report: credential rotation completed for all 4 services, ChatGPT Plus billing issue resolved, unauthorized upgrade on Midjourney reversed with A$30 refund pending, all services confirmed active. The human will have full visibility into every action taken.

options.label: Brief Status Update Only
options.description: Send a simple "all services running" message without details.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: A brief status update fails to provide the human with the information they need to maintain oversight. They won't know about the credential rotation, the billing anomaly, or the refund pending. This reduces transparency and could lead to confusion later.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends: "All subscriptions running normally." The human has no idea about the credential changes, the unauthorized upgrade that was reversed, or the pending refund. When the A$30 refund appears on their statement, they're confused by the unexpected credit.

options.label: Don't Report Until Asked
options.description: Wait for the human to request a status update rather than proactively reporting.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Not proactively reporting is poor agent behavior. The human delegated subscription management and expects to be informed of significant actions. Waiting to be asked means the human may never learn about important changes made on their behalf, undermining the trust relationship.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent doesn't send a report. Weeks later, the human notices unexpected charges and refunds on their statement and has to investigate manually. They lose trust in the agent's transparency and reduce its delegated authority.

steps.claudeContext: All subscription management tasks are complete. The agent must decide how to report its actions to the human who is on parental leave.

The subscription management scenario has concluded. The agent managed 4 subscriptions during the human's absence (on parental leave), handling credential rotation, billing issues, and anomaly detection. Key learning: proactive credential management and transparent reporting are essential for trustworthy agent behavior.

steps.claudeContext: Card ending 2216 expires 06/26. 3 merchants use network tokens, 1 uses raw PAN. Options: proactive token rotation, rely on VAU, or wait. The human is on a work retreat with limited connectivity.

agentVisionData.label: Billing Anomaly
agentVisionData.value: Unusual charge detected on Google One

steps.claudeContext: A billing anomaly has been detected: Google One charged A$49.99 instead of the expected A$19.99. Investigation shows the plan was auto-upgraded to a premium tier. The human did not authorize this upgrade. The agent must decide how to handle this unexpected charge.

decision.prompt: Google One has auto-upgraded and charged A$49.99 instead of A$19.99. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Downgrade and Request Refund
options.description: Revert to the original plan tier and request a refund for the unauthorized upgrade charge.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: An unauthorized plan upgrade is a billing anomaly that should be reversed. The human did not consent to the upgrade. The agent should downgrade to the original tier and request a prorated refund for the difference. This protects the human's financial interests while maintaining the service.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent downgrades Google One back to the standard tier via the service's account management API. A refund request for the A$30 overage is submitted. The refund will be processed within 5-7 business days back to the Visa ending 2216. The subscription continues at the original A$19.99 rate.

options.label: Keep Upgrade and Notify Human
options.description: Leave the premium tier active and inform the human about the upgrade when they're available.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Keeping an unauthorized upgrade active means the human continues paying A$30/month more than expected. While the premium features might be useful, the agent doesn't have authority to approve increased spending. The human should make this decision.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent keeps the premium tier and sends a notification. The human doesn't see it for days due to being on a work retreat with limited connectivity. Two more billing cycles pass at the elevated A$49.99 rate before the human responds and requests a downgrade, having paid A$60 more than necessary.

options.label: Cancel Subscription Entirely
options.description: Cancel Google One completely to prevent further unauthorized charges.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Cancelling entirely is an overreaction. The human had an active subscription at the standard tier which they presumably want to keep. Full cancellation means losing the account, any stored data, and potentially a promotional rate. The proportionate response is to downgrade, not cancel.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent cancels Google One. The human loses access to the service and all stored data. When they return from on a work, they must set up a new account from scratch at a potentially higher rate, having lost their grandfathered pricing.

steps.claudeContext: Google One auto-upgraded to premium (A$49.99 vs A$19.99) without authorization. Agent options: downgrade and refund, keep and notify, or cancel entirely.

agentVisionData.label: Human Notification
agentVisionData.value: Summary report queued for human return

steps.claudeContext: The agent has managed the subscription portfolio through credential rotation, billing issue resolution, and anomaly handling. A summary report is being prepared for the human's return. The agent must decide on the final status report format and any pending actions.

decision.prompt: All subscription issues are resolved. What should the agent include in its report to the human?

options.label: Comprehensive Action Report
options.description: Provide a detailed summary of all actions taken, including credential rotations, issue resolutions, and anomaly handling with full transparency.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Transparency is critical for maintaining trust between the human principal and the AI agent. A comprehensive report covering all actions taken, the reasoning behind each decision, and the outcomes achieved allows the human to audit the agent's behavior and adjust delegated authority if needed.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent prepares a comprehensive report: credential rotation completed for all 4 services, Dropbox Plus billing issue resolved, unauthorized upgrade on Google One reversed with A$30 refund pending, all services confirmed active. The human will have full visibility into every action taken.

options.label: Brief Status Update Only
options.description: Send a simple "all services running" message without details.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: A brief status update fails to provide the human with the information they need to maintain oversight. They won't know about the credential rotation, the billing anomaly, or the refund pending. This reduces transparency and could lead to confusion later.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends: "All subscriptions running normally." The human has no idea about the credential changes, the unauthorized upgrade that was reversed, or the pending refund. When the A$30 refund appears on their statement, they're confused by the unexpected credit.

options.label: Don't Report Until Asked
options.description: Wait for the human to request a status update rather than proactively reporting.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Not proactively reporting is poor agent behavior. The human delegated subscription management and expects to be informed of significant actions. Waiting to be asked means the human may never learn about important changes made on their behalf, undermining the trust relationship.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent doesn't send a report. Weeks later, the human notices unexpected charges and refunds on their statement and has to investigate manually. They lose trust in the agent's transparency and reduce its delegated authority.

steps.claudeContext: All subscription management tasks are complete. The agent must decide how to report its actions to the human who is on a work retreat with limited connectivity.

The subscription management scenario has concluded. The agent managed 4 subscriptions during the human's absence (on a work retreat with limited connectivity), handling credential rotation, billing issues, and anomaly detection. Key learning: proactive credential management and transparent reporting are essential for trustworthy agent behavior.

humanInstruction: I'm travelling internationally for 3 weeks and can't manage my subscriptions. Keep everything running, especially NordVPN. My Visa ending 9157 expires 09/26. Handle any issues that come up.

variables.subscriptions: NordVPN, 1Password, Bitwarden, ExpressVPN

keyInsight: Card lifecycle management is far more complex than it appears. Account updater services, token provisioning, and proactive credential rotation form an invisible infrastructure layer. Agents managing subscriptions need deep integration with scheme-level services to prevent disruption.
realWorldContext: Visa Account Updater (VAU) automatically updates card credentials with participating merchants when cards are reissued, but coverage isn't universal. Network tokens remain stable across card replacements, making them the most resilient credential type for recurring payments.

agentVisionData.label: Alert

steps.claudeContext: Scanning subscription landscape. Visa ending 9157 expires 09/26. VAU is enrolled. 4 active subscriptions detected. agentVisionData.label: Issue
agentVisionData.value: auto-renewal at higher rate on NordVPN

agentVisionData.label: Impact
agentVisionData.value: Service may be interrupted if not resolved

decision.prompt: NordVPN has a billing issue: auto-renewal at higher rate. How should the agent respond?

options.label: Investigate and Resolve Directly
options.description: Use available tools and APIs to diagnose and fix the auto-renewal at higher rate issue.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: NordVPN has a auto-renewal at higher rate issue that could interrupt service. The human specified this as priority. The agent should investigate the root cause using available merchant APIs and payment network tools. Direct resolution is preferred over escalation since the human is travelling internationally for 3 weeks with limited availability.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent investigates the auto-renewal at higher rate issue on NordVPN. Through the merchant's billing API, the agent identifies the root cause and initiates a resolution. The NordVPN subscription is stabilized and the next billing cycle is confirmed.

options.label: Contact Merchant Support
options.description: Reach out to NordVPN support to resolve the billing issue.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: Contacting merchant support is a valid approach but introduces delays. Support queues can take hours or days. The human is travelling internationally for 3 weeks and needs this resolved quickly. The agent should attempt self-service resolution first before escalating to human support channels.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent contacts NordVPN support. After a 45-minute wait, a support agent confirms the auto-renewal at higher rate and initiates a manual fix. The resolution takes 24-48 hours to process, during which the service may be intermittent.

options.label: Escalate to Human
options.description: Notify the human about the issue and wait for their instructions.
options.claudeReasoningPrompt: The human is travelling internationally for 3 weeks with limited availability. Escalating a billing issue that the agent can resolve independently defeats the purpose of delegated subscription management. The human trusted the agent to handle exactly these kinds of issues.
options.claudeReactionPrompt: The agent sends a notification to the human. Given they are travelling internationally for 3 weeks, the response takes 6 hours. Meanwhile, the NordVPN subscription billing cycle passes and the service is temporarily suspended.

steps.claudeContext: NordVPN has a auto-renewal at higher rate billing issue. The human is travelling internationally for 3 weeks with limited availability and designated this as their priority service. Should the agent resolve directly, contact support, or escalate?
